    Jeff Coyle
    Wednesday, Apr 22
    10:00 a.m. Wednesday, Apr 22
    Be Seen by Everyone: Why Accessibility Powers SEO, AEO, and Growth [S725]

    , SVP, Product Strategy & Growth, Siteimprove

    You’re hearing a lot about SEO, AI search, and answer engine optimization (AEO), but what if the biggest reason your content isn’t being found or converting has nothing to do with keywords or tools? Rather strong website fundamentals, including digital accessibility, is the foundation that modern search engines, AI readers, and real people rely on to discover, consume, and act on your content. When accessibility is missing, at least 25% of your audience can’t fully access your digital experiences, and search engines struggle too. Learn how to connect accessibility and governance to higher conversion and stronger SEO and AEO performance while meeting the growing compliance requirements across industries.

    Key takeaways:

    • Use AI to scale accessibility without adding headcount
    • Turn governance and accessibility into a core part of your growth strategy
    • Bring accessibility and governance out of the compliance silo and into the marketer’s toolkit.

    By clicking add to schedule, I agree the Adobe family of companies may share my contact information with Siteimprove for marketing purposes.

    Track: Brand Visibility and Content Management in the Agentic Web, Customer Engagement, AI, Innovation, and Leadership Trends

    12:00 p.m. Wednesday, Apr 22
    Beyond Earn & Burn: The Future of Loyalty Orchestration [CP21]

    , Principal Product Marketing Manager, Adobe

    , Senior Product Manager, Adobe

    Loyalty is evolving beyond points and perks — it’s about creating adaptive, hyper-personalized experiences that keep customers engaged in real time. Discover how Adobe’s new loyalty capabilities in Adobe Experience Platform empower marketers to overcome common challenges like fragmented data, low engagement, and resource constraints. Learn how real‑time insights, AI‑powered automation, and a unified customer view transform loyalty programs from manual and reactive to dynamic and scalable. Explore how modern loyalty strategies leverage gamification and challenge-based experiences to drive higher engagement and stretch behaviors, moving beyond one-size-fits-all offers toward interactive, personalized moments that motivate customers to act. Get real‑world examples from brands who drive faster execution, deeper personalization, and measurable impact. 

    Key takeaways: 

    • Explore the future of loyalty and why it’s shifting toward personalized, adaptive engagement 
    • Learn how Adobe’s loyalty tools unify data, personalize experiences, power gamified challenges, and automate complexity to drive higher engagement and stretch behaviors 
    • See real-world results from brands scaling loyalty tactics 
    Track: Customer Engagement
    Product: Adobe Experience Platform, Adobe Journey Optimizer

    Beyond Silos: Why Enterprise Ops Is the Key to Brand-Agency Alignment [OS809]

    , Sr. Product Marketing Manager, Adobe

    , Group Manager, Product Management, Adobe

    Teams today must deliver standout work faster than ever while proving its impact on the business. Hear how leading brands and agencies are evolving their operating models to protect creativity while improving speed, visibility, and measurable outcomes. Learn the practices high‑performing teams use to optimize talent allocation, streamline approvals, manage financial impact, and collaborate more effectively. See how Adobe Workfront and its advanced enterprise operations capabilities help organizations scale creative output with clarity, efficiency, and governance.

    Key takeaways:

    • How modern brands and agencies like Publicis are evolving their operating models to compete on creativity, speed, and ROI
    • How Adobe Workfront enables smarter resource planning, financial visibility, and AI‑powered workflows
    • What’s coming next on the Workfront roadmap to help teams scale creative work with control and confidence
    Track: Workflow and Planning
    Product: Adobe Workfront

    Sunil Ramakrishnappa

    Monday, Apr 20
    Beyond the Machine: Komatsu’s Global Blueprint for a Unified Experience [S705]

    , Chief Digital Officer, Komatsu

    , Director, Digital Marketing, Komatsu

    , SVP, Solution Consulting, Infosys x Blue Acorn iCi

    , Director, Global Industry Strategy - IM, HT, HLS, PubSec, Auto, Partners, Adobe

    Global manufacturers face mounting digital pressures: fragmented regional sites, inconsistent dealer experiences, siloed data, and customers who expect B2C‑level convenience in every B2B interaction. Many organizations also struggle to turn machine data signals into meaningful insights that fuel smarter service, proactive support, and stronger lifecycle engagement. Michael Gidaspow, Komatsu’s chief digital officer, shares how the company addressed these challenges by launching the Komatsu Experience (KX) program — a unified digital ecosystem built on Adobe Experience Cloud that connects commerce, data, various digital solutions, dealer partners, and machine intelligence. Explore how they shifted from a hardware‑centric business model to a digitally driven, customer‑centric approach that streamlines operations, elevates dealer collaboration, and delivers more personalized, intuitive experiences for every user.

    Key takeaways:

    • How Komatsu unified dealer workflows, commerce, and customer experiences
    • Operational challenges behind global digital transformation
    • How Komatsu is moving beyond selling equipment to solving customer problems

    By clicking add to schedule, I agree the Adobe family of companies may share my contact information with Infosys for marketing purposes.

    Track: Commerce, B2B Customer Journeys, Enterprise Productivity for High-Performing Teams
    Product: Adobe Experience Manager, Adobe Experience Platform, Adobe Experience Cloud, Adobe Commerce

    Tuesday, Apr 21
    Beyond the SKU: How Digital Twins Power Experience and Personalization [CP3]

    , SVP, Value Design & Realization, Infosys x Blue Acorn iCi

    , Head of Digital Strategy Group—Tech, Mfg, B2B, Adobe

    From the factory floor to the digital storefront, companies are drowning in product complexity — variants, regions, and channels — yet most experiences still treat products as static content. Whether you’re a manufacturer or a retailer, it’s time to move beyond the SKU. Adobe’s Stephen Ratpojanakul and Blue Acorn iCi’s Tera Simon reveal how a “digital twin” transforms fragmented product data into a revenue-driving engine across Adobe Experience Cloud. Discover how leading brands use structured product intelligence to power AI-driven asset generation with Adobe Firefly, personalize experiences in Adobe Experience Platform, and measure product engagement through Adobe Customer Journey Analytics. Get examples of how teams activate product data across content, personalization, and performance, reducing content duplication, accelerating launches, and tying product engagement back to real business results.

    Key takeaways:

    • What a digital twin is and why product complexity is breaking digital experiences
    • How product intelligence powers content and personalization with Firefly and Adobe Experience Platform
    • How to connect product engagement to measurable business outcomes with Customer Journey Analytics
    Track: Commerce
    Product: Adobe Experience Platform, Adobe Customer Journey Analytics

    , Sr. Product Manager, Adobe

    , Principal, Product Management, Adobe

    AI agents in Adobe Experience Manager expose a broad number of skills and tools that DAM users can leverage to improve their productivity and integrate with their own agentic workflows. Tasks that used to take hours can be done in minutes all with broad monitoring and audit capabilities. 

    Key takeaways:

    • Learn how to use agents for content management in the DAM
    • Use agents to optimize content activation

    Wednesday, Apr 22
    9:00 a.m. Wednesday, Apr 22
    Bridging the Gap Between Digital and Social Touchpoints [S728]

    , VP Product Management, Sprinklr

    , Director, Social Operations, Response, and Care, Adobe

    AI is redefining how brands connect with customers across digital and social touchpoints, but most organizations are still operating in fragmented systems that separate what happens on owned properties from what happens in the real world of social channels, communities, and modern customer interactions. Anish Chadda, VP of Product at Sprinklr, and Lindsay Munro, director of Social Operations, Response and Care at Adobe, explore how leading enterprises are unifying digital and social experiences to deliver more connected, real-time, and emotionally intelligent customer journeys. Learn how Adobe and Sprinklr’s joint innovation work allows brands to break down channel silos, marketing, service, and content teams to deliver a unified content supply chain across channels. See what a next-generation experience engine looks like with real examples, and how to bring it to life in your own organization through unified journey workflows and actionable frameworks you can deploy immediately.

    Key takeaways:

    • How AI unlocks faster content velocity, personalization, and brand-safe activation across channels
    • Unified AI workflows to reduce operational cost and increase marketing and care efficiency
    • Delivering closed-loop attribution across social and digital to build a 360º customer truth

    By clicking add to schedule, I agree the Adobe family of companies may share my contact information with Sprinklr for marketing purposes.

    Track: B2B Customer Journeys

    Monday, Apr 20
    Bringing It All Together to Modernize Financial Services at Scale [S912]

    , Global Head, Digital Sales & Engagement Journeys, Standard Chartered

    , Sr. Director Global Industry Strategy, Adobe

    , Head of Global Brand & Marketing Technology, Sun Life Financial

    Financial services organizations are large, complex, and highly matrixed — and often risk averse by design. Enterprise-wide change doesn’t fail because of technology; it stalls when alignment across functions, regions, and governance structures breaks down. This session dives into the “how” of driving modernization at scale, focusing on the people, operating models, and processes that make meaningful change stick — from improving experiences to driving measurable impact as institutions factor AI into workflows and future strategies.

    Gain global insights from leaders at Sun Life and Standard Chartered, representing insurance and banking, on how they navigated silos, aligned stakeholders, and modernized processes to improve customer and advisor experiences.
     
    In this session, you will learn about:

    • Key findings from Adobe’s AI and Digital Trends report, highlighting how organizations worldwide are moving beyond experimentation with AI to reshape customer journeys, unify data, and deliver more personalized, relevant experiences that drive growth
    • How Sun Life has moved from a fragmented CX and MarTech stack to an outcome driven, AI orchestrated ecosystem—rationalizing vendors, reorganizing teams around impact, and unlocking adoption to deliver measurable business value.
       
    Track: Customer Data Management and Collaboration, Customer Engagement, AI, Innovation, and Leadership Trends
    Product: Adobe Experience Manager, Adobe Experience Platform, Adobe Experience Cloud, Adobe Real-Time CDP, Adobe Customer Journey Analytics, Adobe Journey Optimizer

    This one-day session is an essential overview course where you'll acquire knowledge across Adobe’s Digital Experience Cloud solutions: Adobe Experience Manager, Data Collection (Tags), Adobe Experience Platform, Adobe Analytics, Adobe Target, and Journey Optimizer.  You'll discover how each solution helps you deliver engaging, real-time experiences to customers, and learn how these solutions work together.

    Build a Dynamic Data Foundation: Adobe’s Own Real-Time CDP Playbook for B2B [S503]

    , Strategic Tech & Data Manager, Adobe

    , Group Product Manager, Adobe

    , Sr. Product Marketing Manager (Real-Time CDP, B2B Edition), Adobe

     

    Modern B2B buying journeys are complex, non-linear, and increasingly real time. Yet most marketing stacks aren’t built to keep up. Adobe’s marketing team shares how it uses Adobe Real-Time CDP B2B Edition to establish a unified data foundation, activate audiences across channels, and build a strategy to scale from targeted pilots to AI-powered journeys. Get examples from Adobe’s high-tech marketing organization across the pre- and post-sales account lifecycle, along with guidance that you can apply across manufacturing, healthcare, and financial services.

    Key takeaways:

    • A framework for where to start, how to scale, and how to prepare your B2B data for the next wave of AI-driven experiences 
    • Tips and tricks to successfully deploy and expand CDP usage effectively
    • Understanding the breadth of CDP use cases available and learning new functionality to extend those use cases
    Track: Customer Data Management and Collaboration, B2B Customer Journeys
    Product: Adobe Experience Platform, Adobe Real-Time CDP, Adobe Journey Optimizer B2B Edition, Adobe Real-Time CDP B2B Edition

    Build An Experience in Adobe Experience Manager the Agentic Way [L335]

    , Sr. Product Manager, Adobe

    , Sr. Computer Scientist, Adobe

    AI-driven experience generation in Adobe Experience Manager reimagines enterprise web development through agentic workflows that compress timelines and amplify team productivity. In this hands-on lab, you'll migrate a website to AEM Edge Delivery Services using the Experience Modernization Agent's generative AI capabilities to handle content extraction, design migration, and block development.

     

    Key Takeaways:
    •    End-to-end migration workflow: Migrate your first page in minutes, import site-wide styles, design individual blocks, then scale with bulk import across multiple pages.
    •    Experience Modernization Console: Explore Adobe's hosted AI-assisted development environment purpose-built for Edge Delivery, allowing you to start prompting in seconds without local setup.
    •    Design system integration: Import design updates directly from Figma, keeping your project implementation synchronized with evolving brand standards.

    Track: Developers , Brand Visibility and Content Management in the Agentic Web
    Product: Adobe Experience Manager Sites, Adobe Experience Manager Assets, Adobe Experience Platform Agent Orchestrator

    Build a Scalable Content Engine with AI by Turning Insights into Assets [CP22]

    , Brand Strategist, Phil Pallen Collective, LLC

    Marketing and customer experience teams manage a constant flow of information — reports, transcripts, briefs, customer feedback, research, and performance data. The challenge is turning all that information into clear direction and channel-ready assets at scale. See how Acrobat Studio, AI Assistant in Adobe Acrobat, and Adobe Express work together to create a scalable content engine. Learn how to extract insights, identify themes, generate new ideas, and produce consistent assets across multiple channels in minutes. The workflow also demonstrates how teams can create informed feedback loops that improve clarity, speed, and consistency across campaigns.

    Key takeaways:

    • Use Acrobat AI to extract insights and opportunities from documents and feedback
    • Build a repeatable workflow that transforms information into channel-ready assets
    • Improve team speed, clarity, and consistency with a scalable content engine
    Track: Enterprise Productivity for High-Performing Teams
    Product: Adobe Document Cloud, Adobe Express, Adobe Acrobat Studio

    Build Conversational Experiences Using AEM Content AI and LLM Apps [L338]

    , Principal Product Manager, Adobe

    , Princ. Product Manager, Adobe

    In today’s AI-first world, users don’t browse — they want to ask and express their need. Whether they start their journey inside platforms like ChatGPT or land directly on your site, they expect fast, intelligent, and personalized responses. In this hands-on lab, learn how to meet your users where they are by building LLM-powered experiences across off-site and on-site touchpoints. Get a clear blueprint for creating seamless, generative AI experiences — from intent to action — across the full digital journey.

    Key takeaways:

    • Build Off-site (ChatGPT Apps) using LLM Apps: expose the same actions users complete on your website — like finding products, checking availability, or booking a test drive — directly inside ChatGPT
    • Build On-site (Content AI Powered-Search API or Agents): create a conversational experience for website visitors to find exactly what they need instantly — driven by your trusted brand content
    Track: Brand Visibility and Content Management in the Agentic Web
    Product: Adobe Experience Platform, Adobe Experience Manager Sites, Adobe Experience Manager Assets, Adobe Brand Concierge, Adobe Customer Journey Analytics

    Build Fast, Secure AI-Powered Integrations with Adobe App Builder [OS605]

    , Senior Product Manager, Adobe

    Enterprises need AI services that are secure, compliant, and ready to scale without weeks of setup. Adobe App Builder offers a serverless runtime with enterprise-grade security, auto-scaling, and direct access to Adobe APIs so that you can stand up AI-powered integrations quickly and reliably. Learn how to connect AI to Adobe workflows for intelligent content analysis, predictive routing and approvals, and conversational interfaces, such as documentation assistants. Follow a live demo that deploys an AI documentation assistant in minutes using a new App Builder capability and real-time logs to validate and debug behavior. Leave with a clear set of patterns and examples that you can adapt to your own Adobe integrations and custom applications using Adobe App Builder.

    Key takeaways:

    • Design secure, compliant AI-powered integrations using Adobe App Builder
    • Map AI capabilities to Adobe use cases like content analysis, routing, and documentation assistants
    • Validate and monitor AI experiences with real-time logs and repeatable deployment patterns
    Track: Developers
    Product: Adobe Experience Manager, Adobe Commerce, Adobe Workfront, Adobe GenStudio for Performance Marketing

    Tuesday, Apr 21
    Building a Flexible Revenue Engine: ESRI’s Transformation with Adobe [OS221]

    , Manager, Marketing Technology, Esri

    , Director IST Marketing Technology, Esri

    Esri completed a major transformation by replacing Salesforce Marketing Cloud Account Engagement (Pardot) with Adobe Journey Optimizer B2B Edition, Adobe Marketo Engage, and Adobe Experience Platform in just seven months. Learn how this transformation strategy enabled rapid migration, delivered immediate value, and created a scalable foundation for growth.

    Explore how Esri moved from a campaign-first mindset to an audience-first approach by unifying data, improving buying group visibility, and modernizing its marketing operations. The team shares what changed operationally, how alignment improved across teams, and how cleaner data and stronger insights unlocked faster, more coordinated engagement. 

    Gain practical guidance to rethink your own approach to data, audiences, and go-to-market execution to drive greater flexibility, speed, and impact.

    Key takeaways:

    • See how Esri migrated from a legacy system to a unified Adobe stack in seven months.
    • Learn how an audience-first strategy improved alignment and engagement across Sales and Marketing
    • Apply practical guidance for unifying data and building a scalable go-to-market foundation.
    Track: B2B Customer Journeys
    Product: Adobe Experience Platform, Adobe Marketo Engage, Adobe Journey Optimizer B2B Edition

    General audience
    Include in embedded catalog
    Wednesday, Apr 22
    9:00 a.m. Wednesday, Apr 22
    Building an Agentic System of Value: Trends Shaping the AI Enterprise   [S817]

    , AI Innovation and GTM, Adobe

    , Global Reinvention & Growth Strategy Lead, Accenture

    Agentic AI marks a structural shift in how enterprises create value, moving from point solution AI capabilities to systems that can reason, decide, and act at scale. The leadership challenge is no longer experimentation, but how to operationalize agentic systems that accelerate decision-making while preserving trust, accountability, and control.

     

    In this session, Chris Duffey from Adobe and Zoe Church from Accenture explore how leading organizations are deploying agentic systems across customer experience and the content supply chain.

     

    Key takeaways

    • How agentic systems are shifting enterprises from AI enablement to decision advantage
    • The core patterns behind scalable agentic architectures, including multi-agent coordination, orchestration, and persistent memory
    • What it takes to institutionalize agentic AI: operating models, governance, and platforms built for trust at scale

    You’ll leave with a clear blueprint for becoming an agentic-ready enterprise — one designed to adapt continuously, manage systemic risk, and translate intelligence into measurable business value.

    Track: AI, Innovation, and Leadership Trends, Content Supply Chain for the AI World, Orchestrating Experiences with AI Agents
    Product: Adobe Experience Platform, Adobe Experience Platform Agent Orchestrator, Adobe LLM Optimizer

    Building and Activating Audiences in Real-Time CDP [TRN04]

    , Sr. Corporate Trainer, Adobe

    Please bring your own device to participate in the hands-on demonstrations during this session. 

     

    In this one-day session, you'll learn how to create simple and advance audiences for targeting and personalizing customer experiences in Real-Time Customer Data Platform. You'll also learn how to create and activate destinations to share these audiences across different platforms.

    Tuesday, Apr 21
    11:00 a.m. Tuesday, Apr 21
    Building an Enterprise DAM and Architecture for a Multi-Brand Ecosystem [S715]

    , Digital Experience Specialist, Champion Homes

    , Director of Solution Consulting, Rightpoint

    Learn how Champion Homes and Rightpoint rebuilt ChampionHomes.com using Adobe Experience Manager and Experience Manager Assets to unify content across brands, improve navigation, fine-tune guided search, and accelerate home discovery. Brayden Orwick from Champion Homes and Aryl Watson from Rightpoint present the asset and content model designed to scale across 1,800+ home models, the Experience Manager Assets workflows that keep content accurate and up to date, and the integrations connecting the admin and retail portals for managing leads, orders, warranties, and documentation. Discover how reusable components and a shared design system enabled the launch of seven child brand sites delivering a high-impact, foundational platform that supports Champion’s growth and elevates the homebuyer experience.

    Key takeaways:

    • How to design an enterprise DAM strategy to support product-like content at scale
    • Best practices for building workflows and governance that reduce manual rework and improve content accuracy
    • Strategies for integrating Experience Manager with admin and retailer portals to support the full product and purchase lifecycle

    By clicking add to schedule, I agree the Adobe family of companies may share my contact information with Rightpoint for marketing purposes.

    Track: Customer Engagement
    Product: Adobe Experience Manager, Adobe Experience Manager Assets
    • Tuesday, Apr 2111:30 AM - 12:30 PM PDT

    Tuesday, Apr 21
    Building an Open Agent Ecosystem Across Enterprise Workflows [S856]

    , Principal Product Manager, Adobe

    Agents deliver real value when they operate across the ecosystem where enterprise work already happens. Explore how Adobe is building an open agent ecosystem that brings together Adobe capabilities, strategic partners, and enterprise platforms to drive measurable customer and business outcomes. Hear about new and evolving partnerships that leverage technologies like A2A and MCP to orchestrate agentic capabilities across the broader enterprise stack, helping teams move faster, reduce friction, and get more value from the tools they already use — and how an ecosystem approach to agents is improving execution across marketing, creative, and operations teams. See how organizations are using agents to streamline handoffs, accelerate cycle times, and improve consistency and quality at scale while maintaining enterprise-grade governance, security, and transparency. 

    Key takeaways: 

    • How the Adobe agent ecosystem connects partners, platforms, and workflows to drive customer outcomes 
    • Real-world examples of agents improving speed, efficiency, and execution across teams 
    • What marketing and IT leaders need to consider to support scale, governance, and long-term value

     

    Track: Orchestrating Experiences with AI Agents
    Product: Adobe Experience Platform Agent Orchestrator
    • Tuesday, Apr 214:30 PM - 5:30 PM PDT
      (Wednesday, Apr 225:00 AM - 6:00 AM IST)

    10:00 a.m. Wednesday, Apr 22
    Building NFL Engagement with Optimization Best Practices in Adobe Target [S527]

    , Multi Solution Business Consultant, Adobe

    , Web Development Specialist, Green Bay Packers

    The Green Bay Packers share how they have refined their optimization program using Adobe Target to better understand fan intent, improve engagement, and drive meaningful business outcomes. Their approach centers on using data-driven experimentation and personalization to enable fans to naturally influence and adapt the site to their individual shopping goals, rather than a one-size-fits-all scenario. Learn how CRO best practices in Adobe Target helped the team to fine-tune prioritization, improve efficiency, and translate results into actionable insights. Explore how selectively introducing more advanced optimization capabilities can expand precision and scale, setting the stage for even more future growth. 

    Key takeaways: 

    • How to structure an experimentation program that scales with confidence 
    • Best practices for prioritizing tests and improving efficiency 
    • Turning reporting into insights that guide next steps
    Track: Customer Engagement
    Product: Adobe Target

    Monday, Apr 20
    Checking In to Customer Journey Analytics: Hilton’s Evolution Story [OS116]

    , Sr. Manager, Digital Analytics Experience, Hilton

    , Sr. Product Marketing Manager, Adobe

    In hospitality, exceptional guest experiences depend on seamless coordination behind the scenes. Data transformation is no different. Hilton’s transition from Adobe Analytics to Adobe Customer Journey Analytics and WebSDK wasn’t just a technical migration. It was an operational transformation across teams, processes, and expectations. Now in hindsight, the things that were most impactful were scalable documentation, a structured and repeatable project migration process, thoughtful expectation-setting, and evolving support models for a global organization. Delve into Hilton’s initiatives designed to address real business needs in real time and reinforce adoption during the transition.

    Walk away with insights to guide your own migration, helping your teams feel supported, your stakeholders stay aligned, and your Customer Journey Analytics investment deliver meaningful value.
    Key takeaways:

    • How to build scalable documentation that supports long-term adoption
    • A practical, repeatable project migration framework
    • Strategies for managing expectations and supporting users through change
    Track: Analytics
    Product: Adobe Analytics, Adobe Customer Journey Analytics

    Cisco Adds Next-Level B2B Analysis with Adobe Customer Journey Analytics [S108]

    , Product Manager, Adobe Analytics, Adobe

    , Sr. Manager Product Management, Adobe

    , Sr. Product Manager, Digital and Ecommerce, Cisco

    Get a real-world demonstration of how Cisco uses Adobe Customer Journey Analytics B2B Edition to unify account, person, and opportunity data and drive deeper insights and faster decisions. Today’s B2B organizations navigate complex buying groups, long sales cycles, and data distributed across CRM, MAP, and digital systems. Customer Journey Analytics B2B Edition brings these sources together for a clearer understanding of engagement across every stage of the customer journey. Discover how new capabilities, such as multiple dimension columns, total population reporting, and account stitching, help teams uncover patterns, improve targeting, and break down internal data silos. Adobe experts and Cisco’s analytics leader share practical approaches to preparing B2B data, aligning cross-functional teams, and accelerating value.

    Key takeaways:

    • See how Cisco unifies account and person data to reveal buying group behavior and accelerate decision making
    • Apply new Customer Journey Analytics B2B capabilities — multiple dimension columns, total population reporting, and account stitching — to deepen analysis and eliminate data blind spots
    • Equip your marketing, sales, and analytics teams with a connected view of engagement across CRM, MAP, and digital channels to drive revenue impact
    Track: Analytics
    Product: Adobe Customer Journey Analytics B2B Edition

    General audience
    Include in embedded catalog
    11:00 a.m. Monday, Apr 20
    Monday, Apr 20
    Clicks to Visibility: How General Motors Builds Brand Presence in AI Search [S331]

    , Director of Product Management, Adobe

    , Group Manager - Product Marketing, Adobe

    , Head of Search Strategy & Activation, MediaOne, GM

    AI-powered search and conversational experiences are reshaping how buyers research, compare, and choose brands. LLMs no longer just surface links - they interpret, recommend, and shape the narrative about your brand before a customer ever visits your site. The question is no longer simply “Did they click?” but “Were we visible, cited, accurate, and trusted in that moment?” Yet most digital experiences were built for human audiences, not agentic ones.
    Join this session to learn how Adobe LLM Optimizer helps General Motors (GM) and other brands understand where and how they appear across LLMs, which signals drive that visibility, and which actionable recommendations can turn those insights into changes across site experiences, content strategy, and the partners shaping new search surfaces.
    We’ll also explore how GM is navigating AI search across national and local dealership experiences, ensuring that when LLMs answer automotive and mobility questions, they surface authoritative and brand-safe GM narratives. We’ll examine the emerging KPIs redefining success - where visibility, citations, and sentiment begin to rival CTRs - and why brands that align SEO, web, PR, social, and partnerships around a unified story will be better positioned for AI-driven discovery.

    Join us to:

    • Learn how LLMs gather, interpret, and synthesize information about your brand across owned and third-party sources.
    • Understand how to influence the narrative created by LLMs while maintaining brand accuracy and safety.
    • See how GM is aligning national and local digital experiences to strengthen visibility in AI-generated answers.
    Track: Brand Visibility and Content Management in the Agentic Web
    Product: Adobe LLM Optimizer
    • Monday, Apr 2011:30 AM - 12:30 PM PDT
      (Tuesday, Apr 2112:00 AM - 1:00 AM IST)
      In-Person

    Connected Data, Connected Experiences: The Experience Platform Difference [CP24]

    , Manager, Platform Solutions Consulting, Adobe

    , Principal Product Manager, Adobe

    Organizations are under increasing pressure to deliver consistent, privacy‑safe, real‑time experiences, yet many still run on fragmented data and channel‑specific systems. The result is disjointed experiences and eroded trust. Adobe Experience Platform offers a practical path to a unified customer foundation by standardizing data, enforcing governance, and making profiles and insights actionable for decisioning and activation across every touchpoint.

    Key takeaways:

    • How Adobe Experience Platform gives your brand a single memory and brain so that every interaction feels like part of one coherent, privacy-safe conversation
    • How the core value pillars of Experience Platform enable a durable and differentiated foundation for consistent experiences and measurable growth
    • Recent and future innovations unlocking greater productivity and efficiency gains with Experience Platform
    Track: Customer Data Management and Collaboration, Analytics, Customer Engagement, Orchestrating Experiences with AI Agents
    Product: Adobe Experience Platform, Adobe Real-Time CDP, Adobe Real-Time CDP Collaboration, Adobe Experience Manager Sites, Adobe Experience Manager Assets, Adobe Mix Modeler, Adobe Content Analytics, Adobe Experience Platform Agent Orchestrator, Adobe Experience Manager Sites Optimizer, Adobe Journey Optimizer Experimentation Accelerator, Adobe Customer Journey Analytics, Adobe Journey Optimizer, Adobe LLM Optimizer

    10:00 a.m. Monday, Apr 20
    Content to Impact: T. Rowe Price Personalizes Experiences Across Channels [S324]

    , Sr. Product Marketing Manager, Adobe

    , VP Global Digital Product & Enablement, T. Rowe Price

    Today’s customer journey spans many different touchpoints – AI chatbots, websites, e-mails and more. But regardless of where engagement happens, it’s the brand experience that ultimately drives meaningful engagement and builds connection.

    Hear how T. Rowe Price evolved from a campaign-centric approach to one that puts the customer at the center – moving beyond one-size-fits-all experiences to dynamic, personalized experiences across channels, including web portal and mobile app.

    By bringing together content, data, and journeys, they’re establishing a scalable foundation that enables teams to move faster and tailor experiences, turning customer interactions into lasting engagement and loyalty.

    In this session, discover:

    • How unifying Adobe Experience Manager and Adobe Journey Optimizer makes it faster and easier to create and deliver consistent, personalized experiences at scale
    • What it takes to bring scalable personalization to life – from structuring modular content for reuse across channels to decisioning for next-best offers
    • Sneak peek into upcoming Adobe Experience Manager and Adobe Journey Optimizer innovations
    Track: Brand Visibility and Content Management in the Agentic Web
    Product: Adobe Experience Manager Sites, Adobe Journey Optimizer

    Sports Marketing
    Yes
    <br><a href="https://business.adobe.com/summit/2026/sessions/converting-fan-loyalty-beyond-the-bandwagon-loyalt-s151.html target=_blank">Watch</a><br>
    General audience
    Include in embedded catalog
    1:00 p.m. Tuesday, Apr 21
    Tuesday, Apr 21
    Converting Fan Loyalty: Beyond the Bandwagon – Loyalty that Outlasts the Big Moment [S151]

    , Indiana Pacers

    Fan loyalty used to be built over generations – today it’s earned in real time with new and younger audiences, setting the standard with more choices, more access, and fewer ties to a single brand or team, making retention just as important as acquisition. This panel, developed in collaboration with Sport Beach, brings together perspectives from leading leagues, teams, brands and athletes - including Tyrese Haliburton, to explore how fandom is evolving and what it takes to build lasting connections in a moment-driven world. When fandom is driven by winning moments and superstar players, it can disappear just as quickly as it arrived. The challenge for teams, leagues, and brands is turning those moments of interest into lasting connection. Learn how organizations are building deeper, more sustainable fan relationships by creating consistent, personalized experiences that extend well beyond the game. Discover what every brand leader can do — give people something to belong to, not just something to buy into. From content and community, to direct engagement, brands continue to give fans a reason to stay connected over time.

    Key takeaways:

    • Explore how sports properties convert peak-moment fans into lifelong loyalists
    • Discover why teams and athletes with the most loyal fanbases invest in the in-between — the rituals, community, and culture that drive lasting connection
    Track: Customer Data Management and Collaboration, Brand Visibility and Content Management in the Agentic Web, Customer Engagement, AI, Innovation, and Leadership Trends
    Product: Not Product-Specific
    • Tuesday, Apr 211:30 PM - 2:30 PM PDT
      (Wednesday, Apr 222:00 AM - 3:00 AM IST)
      In-Person

    Costa Cruises: Reinventing the Guest Journey with Adobe + Accenture [S708]

    , VP Global Marketing, Costa Crociere

    , Travel Lead Italy & Greece, Accenture

    Costa Cruises set out to solve a fundamental challenge: How do you truly put the guest at the center of every decision, moving from communication to true connection? The objective was clear — create a single, reliable view of the guest to overcome silos, and use it to ensure that every customer feels connected wherever and whenever they engage with Costa. In partnership with Adobe and Accenture, Costa replatformed its digital ecosystem around Adobe Experience Platform, with Adobe Real-Time CDP as the enterprise data backbone. The new architecture enables a unified consumer view paired with end-to-end orchestration and measurement across the entire journey, from inspiration and booking to onboard engagement and post-cruise loyalty.

    Key takeaways:

    • How to get and share a clear view of a guest, allowing for tailored experiences, targeted upsells, and measurable revenue impact
    • Unifying data and introducing agile multipod teams spanning CRM,
    Track: Customer Engagement, Content Supply Chain for the AI World
    Product: Adobe Real-Time CDP, Adobe Customer Journey Analytics, Adobe Journey Optimizer

    Create More, Launch Faster, and Stay on Brand with Adobe Express [L421]

    , Sr. Principal Solutions Consultant, Adobe

    , Sr. Transformational Solutions Consultant, Adobe

    Keeping up with today’s always-on marketing demands means producing more content faster without compromising brand standards or slowing down creative teams. In this hands-on lab, work directly in Adobe Express to accelerate campaign production using brand-approved templates, AI-powered tools, and built-in collaboration workflows. Experience how Express empowers marketing teams to create, remix, localize, and publish on-brand content with ease — all within a connected Adobe ecosystem, streamlining your content supply chain.

    Key takeaways:

    • Create and customize campaign-ready content for web, social, and video using Adobe Express templates designed for fast last-mile edits, resizing, and localization
    • Increase productivity with Adobe Firefly, using generative AI to adapt and reuse existing assets while maintaining brand consistency
    • Move content from creation to activation faster by leveraging native integrations across Adobe Experience Cloud

    , Director, Head of Customer Education, Atlassian

    As AI reshapes how learners discover and consume information, organizations are rethinking the purpose and impact of customer academies. Explore the evolving role of academies in driving customer adoption, long-term engagement, and customer retention.     4:00 p.m. Tuesday, Apr 21
    Tuesday, Apr 21
    Data Collaboration in Today’s Customer Experience Landscape [OS512]

    , Sr. Solutions Consultant, Real-Time CDP, Adobe

    Reaching audiences where they are is harder than ever, with fewer signals, limited performance insights, and an ever-growing focus on privacy. While many brands have consolidated their own first-party data, they don’t have to go it alone — strategic data partnerships with publishers, brands, and data partners can unlock powerful brand and performance marketing campaigns. Dive into how leading brands and publishers are collaborating today, via privacy-centric data and clean room and data collaboration technology, to improve audience planning, activation, and measurement across the marketing funnel.

    Key takeaways:

    • How brands and publishers are using data collaboration solutions to improve business outcomes
    • How marketers are approaching data partnerships and organizational alignment
    • How to get started with privacy-centric data collaboration with Adobe
    Track: Customer Data Management and Collaboration
    Product: Adobe Experience Platform, Adobe Real-Time CDP, Adobe Real-Time CDP Collaboration, Adobe Real-Time CDP B2B Edition
    • Tuesday, Apr 214:30 PM - 4:45 PM PDT

    Monday, Apr 20
    Data Mirror and MCP: Modern Connectivity for Customer Journey Analytics [L611]

    , Principal Strategist, Adobe

    , Sr. Software Architect, Adobe

    Elevate Customer Journey Analytics with data synchronized from external systems while leveraging agentic workflows for enhanced and repeatable journey insights. Two capabilities make this possible: Data Mirror for automated data ingress and MCP for agentic connectivity. Data Mirror ingests data from upstream systems with change data capture, with changes propagating automatically into Customer Journey Analytics, keeping your journey analysis aligned with source data without manual intervention or brittle pipelines. Explore how the Customer Journey Analytics MCP server connects agents through natural language prompts instead of complex APIs. From conversational interactions that drive efficiency to data integration with enterprise data warehouses, unlock new possibilities for working with journey data.

    Key takeaways:

    • Implement Data Mirror for automated ingestion and governance from external systems
    • Enable agentic workflows and natural language access through MCP
    • Integrate Customer Journey Analytics seamlessly with your data ecosystem
    Track: Developers , Analytics
    Product: Adobe Experience Platform, Adobe Customer Journey Analytics

    12:00 p.m. Monday, Apr 20
    Monday, Apr 20
    Deliver Connected Experiences with Adobe Experience Platform [OS656]

    , Manager, Platform Solutions Consulting, Adobe

    Delivering consistent, real‑time, and privacy‑safe customer experiences has become a board‑level priority, but many organizations are still constrained by siloed data and channel‑centric systems. These fragmentation challenges lead to inconsistent interactions, operational inefficiencies, and eroded customer trust. Adobe Experience Platform provides a clear, scalable path to enterprise transformation. By unifying data, standardizing governance, and activating real‑time insights across every touchpoint, Experience Platform helps brands operate with a single, trusted view of the customer and make better, faster decisions at scale.

    Key takeaways:

    • How Adobe Experience Platform serves as the enterprise’s unified memory and intelligence layer, enabling every customer interaction to feel consistent, connected, and privacy‑safe
    •  How the Experience Platform core value pillars establish a differentiated foundation for operational efficiency, superior experiences, and measurable business impact
    • A look at the latest and upcoming innovations that drive meaningful efficiency gains for teams and unlock new revenue opportunities
    Track: Customer Data Management and Collaboration, Analytics, Customer Engagement, Orchestrating Experiences with AI Agents
    Product: Adobe Experience Platform, Adobe Real-Time CDP, Adobe Real-Time CDP Collaboration, Adobe Experience Manager Sites, Adobe Experience Manager Assets, Adobe Mix Modeler, Adobe Content Analytics, Adobe Experience Platform Agent Orchestrator, Adobe Experience Manager Sites Optimizer, Adobe Journey Optimizer Experimentation Accelerator, Adobe Customer Journey Analytics, Adobe Journey Optimizer, Adobe LLM Optimizer

      (Tuesday, Apr 211:15 AM - 1:30 AM IST)

    , Principal Instructor, Adobe

    , Sr. Technical Instructor, Adobe

    Engage with your customers effectively throughout their journey by leveraging the capabilities of Adobe Journey Optimizer. In this one-day session, you'll learn how to create personalized messages and audience segments and build and publish customer journeys. 

     

    This course will help prepare you for the Adobe Journey Optimizer Business Practitioner Professional certification exam.

    Wednesday, Apr 22
    9:00 a.m. Wednesday, Apr 22
    Design AI-Powered Experiences with MCP, Agent-to-Agent Orchestration, and Skills [S604]

    , Senior Principal Architect, Adobe

    Learn how to design AI-powered experiences using the emerging agent integration stack of Model Context Protocol (MCP), agent-to-agent (A2A) orchestration, and composable Skills. This session builds a practical mental model for how these layers fit together, when to use each one, and how to mix them safely in production. See how leading tools like Claude Code, Cowork, Codex, and Antigravity plug into this stack, explore common patterns and anti-patterns, and walk away with a decision framework that helps your teams ship reliable, extensible AI features faster.
    Key takeaways

    • Understand the core roles of MCP, A2A orchestration, and Skills in the AI integration stack
    • Identify proven design patterns and anti-patterns for connecting agents to tools and to each other
    • Apply a simple decision framework to choose the right integration approach for your next AI use case
    Track: Developers , Orchestrating Experiences with AI Agents
    Product: Adobe Experience Manager, Adobe Experience Platform Agent Orchestrator
    • Wednesday, Apr 229:00 AM - 10:00 AM PDT
      (9:30 PM - 10:30 PM IST)

    1:00 p.m. Wednesday, Apr 22
    Wednesday, Apr 22
    Design for Scale: Practical Adobe Marketo Optimization Strategies [L220]

    , Sr. Field Engineer, Adobe

    , Sr. Field Engineer, Adobe

    Move fast in Marketo Engage without sacrificing performance or data quality. This hands-on lab brings together proven best practices, platform guardrails, and practical exercises to help you optimize your instance for long-term scalability. Learn how to identify and resolve inefficient smart campaigns, design high-performing Smart Lists, work confidently within product limits and data retention policies, and reduce the impact of bot-driven engagement. Working in your own Marketo instance or a demo, explore tools like Campaign Inspector, apply naming and archiving frameworks, and evaluate real-world examples of effective versus problematic operational designs. Leave with a repeatable optimization framework, a self-assessment workbook, and guardrails that you can apply to improve performance, control costs, and strengthen reporting accuracy and ROI insights.

    For this hands-on lab, bring your laptop and a charging cord. Make sure that you can access your organization’s Marketo instance and confirm with your IT department that you’re authorized to log in to your Marketo Engage instance using the conference center’s Wi-Fi. You can work in a sandbox or production.

    Key takeaways:

    • Apply field-tested methods to optimize Marketo Engage performance
    • Identify and remediate Smart List inefficiencies, data growth issues, and retention risks
    • Monitor and maintain database health using reporting and governance best practices
    Track: B2B Customer Journeys
    Product: Adobe Marketo Engage
    • Wednesday, Apr 221:30 PM - 3:00 PM PDT
      (Thursday, Apr 232:00 AM - 3:30 AM IST)

    Tuesday, Apr 21
    Designing Marketo Engage for Scale: Inside Adobe’s Revenue Engine [OS223]

    As organizations grow, marketing automation becomes more complex and more fragile. Lifecycle confusion, misaligned MQL definitions, CRM sync issues, and reporting gaps quickly erode trust across revenue teams. Discover the architecture and systems that turn marketing automation into predictable growth. Marketo architecture is designed for enterprise scale across regions, business units, and revenue motions. Learn how lifecycle stages are system-enforced, CRM sync is governed with precision, and standardized campaign systems prevent operational sprawl. Explore how we connect marketing activity directly to pipeline impact through cross-channel orchestration, SLA automation, and executive-level reporting.

    Key takeaways:

    • Designing system-enforced lifecycle and CRM governance
    • Deploying scalable campaign systems instead of one-off builds
    • Building governance models that increase speed and reporting trust
    Track: B2B Customer Journeys
    Product: Adobe Marketo Engage, Adobe Marketo Measure

    Designing Total Experience for an Outcome-Driven World Shaped by Agentic AI [S719]

    , Vice President, Digital Business Services, HCLTech

    , Senior Vice President, Digital Business Services, HCLTech

    As AI accelerates the pace of business, experience has emerged as the key lever for differentiation and growth. Traditional, reactive experience models struggle to keep up with rising expectations for speed, personalization and consistency across channels. Explore how organizations can design intelligent experiences by embedding AI across customer, employee, and digital journeys. By infusing AI into how experiences are conceived, created, and delivered, enterprises can evolve from static, linear journeys to adaptive systems that respond in real time. Get a strategic view of how AI-powered, total experience-led transformation can unlock speed, scale, and precision while driving sustainable, measurable growth.

    Key takeaways:

    • How experience is evolving from reactive journeys to predictive, intelligent systems
    • Why AI must extend beyond touchpoints into the full design and marketing value chain
    • What leaders must change in strategy, operating models, and platforms to scale intelligent experiences

    By clicking add to schedule, I agree the Adobe family of companies may share my contact information with HCLTech for marketing purposes.

    Include in embedded catalog
    Charlie Sutton
    4:00 p.m. Monday, Apr 20
    Monday, Apr 20
    Design: What Actually Makes AI Scale Across the Enterprise [S747]

    , Chief Design Officer, Atlassian

    , Service Management Evangelist, Atlassian

    Everyone is racing to add AI into customer experiences and the flow of work. Far fewer are redesigning how work actually happens around it. This gap is where transformation stalls: humans and AI need to operate effectively together to navigate the messy reality of cross-functional work. Without intentional design, AI can add friction, widen performance gaps, and stall in pockets of productivity that don't move the needle for the business.

    In this fireside chat, Atlassian Chief Design Officer Charlie Sutton shares why design—not just technology—is the deciding factor in turning AI into the driver of better performance. Charlie will explore how thoughtful design makes human-AI collaboration more intuitive, trustworthy, and effective across teams, and why the organizations that rethink how people, knowledge, and work come together will be the ones that scale AI successfully across the enterprise.

    Key takeaways:

    • Design AI interactions that feel intuitive and trustworthy for employees and customers
    • Redesign cross-functional workflows so humans and AI share work seamlessly, without added friction
    • Re-architect how people, knowledge, and work are organized so AI can scale to enterprise-wide impact
       

    By clicking add to schedule, I agree the Adobe family of companies may share my contact information with Atlassian for marketing purposes.

     

    Track: AI, Innovation, and Leadership Trends
    Product: Not Product-Specific

      (Tuesday, Apr 215:00 AM - 6:00 AM IST)

    DICK's Sporting Goods Reinvents Customer Experience with Brand Concierge [S332]

    , Director, Solution Architect, Adobe

    , Sr. Director of Product and Design, Data & AI, DICK'S Sporting Goods

    , Principal, Product Marketing, GenAI, Brand Concierge, Adobe

    , Director - Product & Design, Mobile Apps, DICK'S Sporting Goods

    Customer expectations are evolving rapidly as AI reshapes how people discover products, ask questions, and make decisions. Adobe Brand Concierge brings together trusted brand content, first-party customer data, and agentic AI to power personalized conversational experiences across digital properties. Get an inside look at how Brand Concierge works and the benefits it delivers. Hear how DICK's Sporting Goods used Brand Concierge to transform its mobile app customer experience to enable more intuitive product discovery, strengthen customer relationships, and drive greater loyalty and conversion.

    Key takeaways:

    • How Adobe Brand Concierge works and what makes it different from competing approaches
    • How to use Brand Concierge to create trusted, personalized interactions while unlocking greater value from your existing Adobe investments
    • How DICK's Sporting Goods is transforming its mobile app experience with Brand Concierge and delivering deeper personalization and rich, multimodal conversations based on customer intent
    Track: Brand Visibility and Content Management in the Agentic Web
    Product: Adobe Brand Concierge

    Wednesday, Apr 22
    8:00 a.m. Wednesday, Apr 22
    Drive Agentic Shopping Experiences from Your Product Catalog [L321]

    , Principal Product Manager, Adobe

    , Sr. Product Manager, Adobe

    As AI-powered search, assistants, and LLM-driven shopping experiences reshape commerce, traditional product catalogs are struggling to keep pace. Disconnected data, inconsistent metadata, and limited real-time visibility reduce product discoverability, fragment shopper journeys, and limit optimization across AI-driven channels. Explore how Adobe unifies catalog data, storefront behavior, and AI tooling into a single commerce architecture. By connecting your catalog to Adobe’s AI ecosystem and leading LLMs, see how Adobe Brand Concierge, Adobe LLM Optimizer, and Sites Optimizer work together to amplify catalog value, power guided shopping experiences, and continuously optimize performance. Learn how to enrich and activate catalog data so that across any channel or AI-driven interface, your products surface with the right price, inventory availability, and product information at the right moment.

    Key takeaways:

    • Improve product visibility across AI-driven search and discovery experiences by aligning catalog data with shopper intent
    • Deliver guided, brand-consistent shopping experiences by enriching product metadata, imagery, and content using Adobe Brand Concierge
    • Monitor live storefront performance to identify engagement or performance gaps and deploy optimizations directly into production with Sites Optimizer
    Track: Commerce
    Product: Adobe Commerce, Adobe Experience Manager Sites, Adobe Brand Concierge, Adobe Experience Manager Sites Optimizer, Adobe Commerce Optimizer, Adobe LLM Optimizer

      (9:00 PM - 10:30 PM IST)

    Include in embedded catalog
    11:00 a.m. Monday, Apr 20
    Monday, Apr 20
    Drive Growth with AI-Enabled Workflows in Adobe GenStudio for Performance Marketing [OS409]

    , Product Strategy & Marketing Manager, Adobe

    High-growth marketing organizations are under constant pressure to scale performance quickly while maintaining speed, brand governance, and measurable ROI.Learn how Adobe GenStudio for Performance Marketing enables teams to assemble, activate, and optimize high-performing content using AI-powered workflows. Explore how marketers and creatives unlock more content variations, reduce creative bottlenecks, and improve paid media results across channels. Designed to deliver immediate value as a standalone application while integrating seamlessly across your company’s broader marketing stack, GenStudio for Performance Marketing helps you move fast today and support more advanced workflows as your teams and business continue to grow.  

    Key takeaways: 

    • See how AI-powered workflows automate ad assembly and refresh cycles, enabling continuous experimentation and stronger marketing ROI 
    • Learn how to turn performance insights into new ad variations, all within a single, streamlined application 
    • Discover how GenStudio for Performance Marketing extends across your marketing stack, amplifying existing tools and enabling scalable workflows over time
    Track: Content Supply Chain for the AI World
    Product: Adobe GenStudio for Performance Marketing

    Tuesday, Apr 21
    11:00 a.m. Tuesday, Apr 21
    Driving Commerce in 2026: Replatforming as a Practice [S734]

    , President, Foreword

    , VP - IT Solutions & Delivery, Dorman Products

    , CTO, Huge

    , Principal Product Marketing Manager, Adobe

    This session explores how Huge partnered with B2B organizations; CDF and Dorman, to modernize complex commerce ecosystems by replatforming and implementing Adobe Commerce. Through practical examples, you’ll learn how Huge helped each organization identify inefficiencies in search, discovery, and purchasing flows, and translate those findings into clear requirements for improving marketplace usability and performance. We’ll review the architecture choices behind these transformations, Adobe Commerce, Logik.io, Algolia, and custom middleware and discuss why each component was selected. The session outlines how configuration speeds were improved, how search was optimized for large catalogs, and how structured processes reduced manual errors.

    You’ll leave with insights into evaluating platform options, improving system performance, and coordinating multiple tools within a B2B commerce environment along with lessons CDF & Dorman learned throughout the digital transformation process.Key Learning Points:

    • How to define requirements that reflect real user and operational needs.
    • How architecture decisions improve not only website performance but operations performance and enable business for growth, scalability, flexibility and innovations
    • Define requirements and scalable solutions for complex product configurations and complex catalog search
    • Solving high-complexity integration challenges with custom middleware solutions.

    By clicking add to schedule, I agree the Adobe family of companies may share my contact information with Huge for marketing purposes.

    Track: Commerce, B2B Customer Journeys
    Product: Adobe Commerce

      (Wednesday, Apr 2212:00 AM - 1:00 AM IST)

    Driving Continuous Marketing Innovation Through Merkle’s Connected Platforms [S922]

    , Principal Enterprise Architect, Adobe

    , Chief Technology Officer, Americas, Merkle

    Discover how Merkle, a dentsu company, accelerated its enterprise-wide transformation and unlocked new marketing capabilities through a connected platform strategy. By unifying data, content, and activation tools, Merkle built a scalable foundation that fuels continuous activation, smarter decision-making, and faster delivery of high-impact use cases.

    What began as a technology initiative evolved into a cultural and operational shift — changing how teams collaborate, experiment, and measure success. This session explores Merkle's journey to operationalize innovation at scale, aligning marketing, data, and technology teams around shared goals and measurable outcomes.

    Attendees will gain practical insights into building a connected foundation that supports agility, experimentation, and AI readiness. Learn how this approach transforms enterprise marketing from siloed execution to continuous innovation that drives business growth and customer value.

    Key Takeaways:

    • Learn how a connected tech foundation accelerates marketing adoption and performance
    • See how unified data and content enable teams to deliver use cases faster
    • Understand how an innovation framework drives ongoing optimization and measurable results
    Track: Content Supply Chain for the AI World
    Product: Adobe Analytics, Adobe Experience Manager, Adobe Target, Adobe Workfront, Adobe GenStudio, Adobe Customer Journey Analytics

    1:00 p.m. Tuesday, Apr 21
    Tuesday, Apr 21
    Driving Growth Through Connected, Data-Driven B2B Journeys at Red Hat [S205]

    , Director, Digital + Orchestration Architecture, Red Hat

    , Director, Product Owners & Delivery - Marketing Technology - CBP, Red Hat

    As Marketing and IT teams strive to deliver seamless B2B experiences, the pressure to align data, technology, and strategy has never been more critical. Red Hat transformed a fragile, disconnected stack into a unified ecosystem by migrating from Eloqua to Adobe Marketo Engage and building a strong data foundation with Adobe Real‑Time CDP.

    Learn how Red Hat cleaned and unified data, expanded marketable audiences by 116%, and implemented AI‑powered lead scoring to improve conversion and sales prioritization. These unified audiences then fueled omnichannel B2B nurture journeys across Adobe Marketo Engage and Adobe Target, creating aligned, personalized experiences and paving the way for advanced account orchestration with Adobe Journey Optimizer B2B Edition.

    Key Takeaways:

    • A strong data foundation with Adobe Real-Time CDP transforms fragile stacks into scalable B2B ecosystems.
    • Unified audiences unlock personalized B2B experiences across channels.
    • Connected orchestration across Marketo Engage, Real-Time CDP, and Adobe Journey Optimizer B2B Edition drives measurable revenue impact - turning technology, marketing, and sales alignment into measurable revenue outcomes.
    Track: B2B Customer Journeys
    Product: Adobe Target, Adobe Marketo Engage, Adobe Real-Time CDP, Adobe Journey Optimizer B2B Edition, Adobe Real-Time CDP B2B Edition

    Driving Impact with Adobe Real-Time CDP Collaboration [L517]

    , Principal Product Manager, Adobe

    , Sr. Manager, Expert Solutions Consulting, RT-CDP, Adobe

    Explore how Adobe Real-Time CDP Collaboration empowers brands and publishers to drive joint audience analysis, activation, and measurement while prioritizing privacy. Learn how to work seamlessly with trusted partners, agencies, and publishers to enhance advertising and co-marketing efforts across channels, like Connected TV, commerce media, and walled gardens. Discover how Real-Time CDP Collaboration minimizes raw data movement, enabling faster operations and stronger privacy compliance without the need for custom data clean rooms. 

    Key takeaways: 

    • Learn about our minimal data movement approach to data collaboration 
    • Explore Real-Time CDP Collaboration from the POV of an advertiser by creating a profile, onboarding audiences, connecting with publishers, and running audience overlaps 
    Track: Customer Data Management and Collaboration

    Elevated Brand Experience: Orchestrating Discoverability for the Agentic Web [SK2]

    , VP of Product & Strategy, Adobe

    , VP of Product, Design & Analytics, DICK'S Sporting Goods

    , VP, Marketing Transformation, DICK'S Sporting Goods

    , Sr. Director, Product Management, Adobe

    , Principal Product Marketing Manager, Adobe

    AI is increasingly shaping how brands are experienced, trusted, and chosen. As customers interact with AI assistants in their everyday lives, expectations for discovery, engagement, and decision-making are changing. To succeed in this environment, brands must move beyond static content and isolated interactions, delivering guided, conversational experiences that reflect intent in the moment while maintaining control over voice, accuracy, and compliance across owned and third-party environments.

     

    In this Strategy Keynote, you’ll hear from Adobe experts and industry leaders on how brands can thrive in the agentic era by orchestrating intelligent content, trust signals, and real-time insights into cohesive experiences. See demonstrations that show how brands can guide customers with confidence, remain in control of how they are represented, and carry intent forward through connected experiences that lead naturally to action across every customer interaction.

    Track: Brand Visibility and Content Management in the Agentic Web, AI, Innovation, and Leadership Trends, Orchestrating Experiences with AI Agents , Enterprise Productivity for High-Performing Teams
    Product: Adobe Experience Manager, Adobe Experience Platform, Adobe Experience Cloud, Adobe Experience Manager Sites, Adobe Experience Manager Assets, Adobe Experience Manager Forms, Adobe GenAI, Adobe Brand Concierge, Adobe Experience Platform Agent Orchestrator, Adobe Experience Manager Sites Optimizer, Adobe Commerce Optimizer, Adobe Experience Manager Guides, Adobe LLM Optimizer, Adobe Marketing Agent for Microsoft 365 Copilot

      (Wednesday, Apr 2212:00 AM - 1:00 AM IST)

    Joe Crawford
    4:00 p.m. Monday, Apr 20
    Monday, Apr 20
    Ensuring Trust Across Financial Experiences in the AI World [S726]

    , Global Head of Solutions Engineering, Glassbox

    , Sr Director - Application Development, Ameriprise Financial Inc

    The rapid adoption of AI technologies is reshaping how users interact with the digital world, raising new questions around authenticity, accuracy, and trust. From AI hallucinations to uncertainty about content ownership, people can be overwhelmed. For financial services, where trust is foundational, delivering reliable digital experiences across channels has never been more critical. User trust is directly correlated with UI/UX errors, complaints, behavioral struggles, accessibility gaps, and fraud exposure. Learn how leading financial institutions uncover trust-eroding moments across digital journeys and turn user behavior into actionable insight. Joe Crawford, global head of Solutions Engineering at Glassbox, shares practical strategies for identifying UX issues, behavioral friction, accessibility gaps, and fraud risks that impact customer confidence.

    Key takeaways:

    • Understand the importance of trust in digital experiences
    • Ways to connect behavioral data to trusted outcomes
    • A real-world example of building digital trust at enterprise scale

    By clicking add to schedule, I agree the Adobe family of companies may share my contact information with Glassbox for marketing purposes.

    Track: Analytics, Customer Engagement, AI, Innovation, and Leadership Trends
    Product: Not Product-Specific

    , Team Manager Personalized Offer - Customer Marketing, Telefónica Germany GmbH & Co. OHG

    , Sr. Enterprise Architect, Adobe

    , Sr. Data Engineer - Customer Data Management, Telefónica Germany GmbH & Co. OHG

    Telefónica Germany is redefining personalized customer engagement by unifying data and orchestration across channels with Adobe Real-Time CDP and Adobe Journey Optimizer. By establishing a centralized omnichannel engagement hub, the organization delivers consistent, relevant experiences at scale, turning customer insights into measurable business impact. Hear how Telefónica Germany reduced shopping cart abandonment through coordinated engagement across digital, retail, and service channels. Get insights on how Telefónica evolved its customer journeys as new channels and capabilities were introduced, aligned cross-functional teams around shared goals, and partnered with Adobe Support & Services to accelerate execution and scale success.

    Key takeaways:

    • How to define high-impact use cases and objectives to deliver consistent, personalized experiences across channels at scale 
    • Why unified data and orchestration are critical to measurable outcomes
    • How the right operating model and partners accelerate transformation

    Experience Rewired: How AI Is Closing the Gap for Customers and Fans [S713]

    , Vice President of Direct-to-Consumer Strategy & Growth, NFL

    , Co-Founder, Executive Chairman, Code and Theory

    , VP, GenAI New Business, Adobe

    For the first time, the tools exist to align what people expect with what they actually experience. Across sports and business, AI is moving from a layer on top to the system that connects everything in real time.

    Hall of Famer Terrell Owens brings a perspective on how access and engagement have fundamentally changed. The NFL shares how it is building more personal, differentiated fan experiences at scale. And Code and Theory outlines how connected systems are turning content, data and context into coordinated action.

    This session explores what happens when audiences, data and timing operate as one system and what that shift means for the people at the center of it: customers and fans.

    Key takeaways:

    • What’s driving the shift from fragmented interactions to connected experiences
    • How to build brand value in an environment defined by content abundance
    • The emerging role of agents in shaping customer experience
    • How the NFL is using a segment-driven model to drive sustained growth

    By clicking add to schedule, I agree the Adobe family of companies may share my contact information with Code and Theory for marketing purposes.

    11:00 a.m. Monday, Apr 20
    Monday, Apr 20
    Fast-Tracking CDP Value: Proven Paths to Adoption, Scale, and ROI [S501]

    , Product Marketing Director, Adobe

    , Product Manager, MarTech Products, Vanguard

    Customer Data Platforms promise powerful personalization and real-time engagement—but many organizations struggle to move from implementation to impact. The difference between stalled initiatives and measurable ROI often comes down to how quickly teams can operationalize, adopt, and scale their CDP investment.

     

    In this session, hear how the team at Vanguard is accelerating Adobe Real-Time CDP adoption by aligning data, teams, and activation from day one. We’ll explore practical strategies for driving faster time to value, avoiding common implementation pitfalls, and enabling teams to confidently activate data across channels. Learn how the right architectural choices, operating models, and use-case prioritization can help organizations move beyond experimentation and deliver tangible business outcomes sooner.

     

    You’ll leave with actionable guidance for building momentum early, scaling what works, and turning your CDP into a durable growth engine.

    • Learn how to accelerate CDP adoption by prioritizing high-impact use cases that deliver measurable value early 
    • Understand the architectural and operating model choices that reduce time to value while supporting long-term scale 
    • Identify common pitfalls that slow CDP activation—and how leading teams avoid them 
    • Gain practical guidance for turning data into action quickly, enabling teams to move from implementation to outcomes with confidence

     

    Track: Customer Data Management and Collaboration
    Product: Adobe Audience Manager, Adobe Experience Platform, Adobe Real-Time CDP
    • Monday, Apr 2011:30 AM - 12:30 PM PDT
      (Tuesday, Apr 2112:00 AM - 1:00 AM IST)

    Monday, Apr 20
    From Agents to Outcomes: Reimagine Enterprise DAM [OS337]

    , Product Marketing Manager - AEM Assets, Adobe

    In today’s omnichannel landscape, enterprise DAM is the strategic engine driving content velocity, governance, and brand consistency. As agentic capabilities reshape DAM, organizations can automate enrichment, streamline compliance, and deliver intent-driven recommendations —empowering teams to reduce manual effort and risk. Explore the key pillars of a modern DAM — unified discovery, embedded governance, automated optimization, omnichannel delivery, and actionable insights. Learn how these pillars enable seamless collaboration, maximize content value, and future-proof digital operations for the agentic era, transforming how leaders and practitioners orchestrate content across every touchpoint.

    Track: Brand Visibility and Content Management in the Agentic Web

    Monday, Apr 20
    From Batch to Real Time: What You Can Do with Marketo Engage Data Streams [OS220]

    , Sr. Product Marketing Manager, Adobe Marketo Engage, Adobe

    , Director, Global Marketing Automation & Data Management, Workday

    Learn how data streams let you send activity data directly to your systems without polling, batch exports, or delays. Hear firsthand how Adobe’s most data-driven customers are using data streams to power advanced integrations, reduce API strain, and trigger automation the moment an event occurs. Whether you’re looking to scale your ops or just tired of waiting for data syncs, discover how to make your marketing operations faster, smarter, and easier to scale. 

    Key takeaways: 

    • Learn how Marketo Engage data streams provide real-time access to lead, asset, and system events 
    • Understand how data streams reduce API usage and replace polling and batch workflows 
    • Explore practical automation patterns using EventBridge, Adobe Runtime, and custom webhooks 
    Track: B2B Customer Journeys
    Product: Adobe Marketo Engage

    Monday, Apr 20
    10:00 a.m. Monday, Apr 20
    From Blind Spots to Bright Spots: Collaboration for Brands and Agencies [CP20]

    , Principal, Product Marketing, Real-Time CDP & Collaboration, Adobe

    , SVP Product Management, Epsilon / Publicis Groupe

    With shrinking marketing budgets, signal loss, and rising privacy expectations, brands and agencies need effective ways to deliver omnichannel campaigns to the right audiences and deliver measurable business outcomes. But often, existing channel and data siloes mean agencies and brands are faced with built-in blind spots that lead to wasted spend and missed opportunities to convert customers. Adobe creates the missing connection between paid media activations and owned channel experiences. Learn how Adobe unlocks privacy-first audience collaboration and outcomes-based measurement to drive better planning, activation, and performance. Whether you’re a data leader, marketer, or agency, gain ideas to supercharge your marketing and advertising strategy with durable first-, second-, and third-party data.

    Key takeaways:  

    • Insight into new ways of working to drive prospecting and retargeting  
    • How Adobe enables audience planning, activation, and measurement with full-funnel data collaboration  
    • Guidance for agencies and brands to collaborate with publishers, data partners, and brand partners for deeper insights 
    Track: Customer Data Management and Collaboration
    Product: Adobe Experience Platform, Adobe Real-Time CDP, Adobe Real-Time CDP Collaboration, Adobe Real-Time CDP B2B Edition
    • Monday, Apr 2010:15 AM - 10:40 AM PDT

    11:00 a.m. Monday, Apr 20
    Monday, Apr 20
    From Bottlenecks to Breakthroughs: BMS’s AI-Led Marketing Transformation [CP4]

    , VP-Commercialization AI Strategy and Analytics, BMS

    , CEO Accenture Song, Accenture

    Behind every marketing campaign are multiple steps — planning, content creation, segmentation, approvals, launch, and optimization. Bristol Myers Squibb saw an opportunity to remove friction across this complex ecosystem and fundamentally reimagine how marketing operates by harnessing the power of AI. BMS shares how it transformed its marketing engine from fragmented workflows into a scalable, AI-powered model. By reshaping its content supply chain, accelerating end-to-end campaign execution, and enabling more data-driven engagement, BMS is shifting marketing teams from executional bottlenecks to strategic impact and driving measurable business outcomes along the way.

    Key takeaways:

    • Through a real-world example, Bristol Myers Squib outlines where it started and where it is today
    • How BMS made the shift
    • The metrics that define success
    Track: AI, Innovation, and Leadership Trends, Content Supply Chain for the AI World
    Product: Adobe Analytics, Adobe Experience Manager, Adobe Workfront, Adobe Experience Manager Assets, Adobe Firefly

    1:00 p.m. Tuesday, Apr 21
    Tuesday, Apr 21
    From Chaos to Orchestration: Transforming the Content Supply Chain [S402]

    , Director, Product Management - GenStudio, Adobe

    , Global President, Adobe Practice, Dentsu

    , Chief Future Officer, Dentsu

    AI is reshaping what marketing teams can produce, but for many organizations, it’s not solving the content challenge. In fact, when creative workflows remain disconnected from media, customer experience, and data, generating more assets faster can amplify broken processes and outdated operating models. The brands that win aren’t those that adopt AI first but those that use this moment to transform how they work by reimagining roles and processes, connecting creation to activation, and applying the discipline to know where to start. 
     
    Dentsu and Adobe share what real-world transformation looks like, drawing on Dentsu’s proprietary Content Supply Chain Maturity Audit — used by enterprise clients in various industries.
    Key takeaways:

    • See how organizations assess their maturity across five critical dimensions and why the right starting point is different for every team
    • Understand the four maturity stages and the organizational, creative, and technology moves required to advance through each
    • Discover how to transform your content supply chain with smarter workflows, operating models, and technology
    Track: Content Supply Chain for the AI World
    Product: Adobe GenStudio

    Michael Schanker
    Wednesday, Apr 22
    10:00 a.m. Wednesday, Apr 22
     From Complexity to Commerce: How Retail Media Scales on Trust  [S733]

    , CMO, OneTrust

    Retail media is one of the fastest‑growing revenue opportunities in marketing—but for large enterprises, it starts with complexity: multiple brands, shared loyalty programs, legacy stacks, and rising expectations for transparency and control.

    In this session, OneTrust CMO Michael Schanker and Canadian Tire Corporation SVP Simone Lumsden show how consent and preferences become the control layer that lets retail media scale. Learn how CTC activates consented first‑party data across loyalty and retail media networks, integrates with Adobe Real‑Time CDP, and enables clean room collaboration to drive higher‑value audiences, closed‑loop measurement, and new monetization.

    The takeaway: trust‑based data isn’t overhead—it’s revenue infrastructure for modern martech stacks.

    By clicking add to schedule, I agree the Adobe family of companies may share my contact information with OneTrust for marketing purposes.

    Track: Customer Data Management and Collaboration, Orchestrating Experiences with AI Agents
