    Jerry Roper
    4:00 p.m. Tuesday, Apr 21
    Tuesday, Apr 21
    From Content Chaos to Global Content Supply Chain [OS755]

    What does it take to deliver one consistent digital experience across 71 sites and 33 languages without slowing local teams down? Deloitte Digital shares its global content transformation, which included a full redesign and migrating 150,000 pages. Some of the long-term benefits included reducing content by 65% and removing 98% of the custom code while greatly improving site performance and digital engagement. Adobe and Deloitte Digital collaborated to unlock the vision with an end-to-end content supply chain that connects planning, production, delivery, and performance measurement. We’ll walk through the business needs behind each stage — from accelerating kickoffs and approvals to assembling content into personalized experiences — and how Adobe tools enabled the operating model, not just the website.

    Key takeaways:

    • Practical ways to accelerate review, approval, and governance with speed
    • How to streamline activation: findability, reuse, and dynamic delivery

    By clicking add to schedule, I agree the Adobe family of companies may share my contact information with Deloitte Digital for marketing purposes.

    Track: B2B Customer Journeys, Brand Visibility and Content Management in the Agentic Web, Content Supply Chain for the AI World
    Product: Adobe Experience Manager, Adobe Workfront, Adobe Experience Manager Sites

    Jake Bennett
    4:00 p.m. Tuesday, Apr 21
    Tuesday, Apr 21
    From Content to Conversation: Activating the AI‑Powered Content Supply Chain [S710]

    , Head of MarTech Platforms, Vanguard

    , SVP Adobe Americas Alliance Leader, Merkle

    Financial institutions like Vanguard have world‑class content engines, but most still operate fragmented content supply chains—resulting in static, one‑size‑fits‑all experiences that no longer meet customer expectations for relevance, immediacy, or personalization. With service costs rising, attention spans shrinking, and customers demanding real‑time digital guidance, modernizing the content supply chain has become a business imperative.

     

    In this session, Vanguard and Merkle reveal how they are transforming their content supply chain into an AI‑powered conversation engine. By embedding AI‑driven decisioning, dynamic content assembly, and automated variation creation across the supply chain—from intake and orchestration to delivery—Vanguard is shifting content from broadcast to true dialogue. The result: real‑time, 1:1 experiences delivered at enterprise scale without scaling people.

     

    You’ll see how Vanguard activates this model using Adobe technologies—including AEM, Firefly, and AI‑powered decisioning—within a broader CSC operating framework. We’ll break down the governance model, content architecture, and testing approach that make AI‑driven personalization not just theoretically possible, but operationally repeatable, safe, and compliant in a regulated environment.

     

    By clicking add to schedule, I agree the Adobe family of companies may share my contact information with Dentsu for marketing purposes.

    Track: Content Supply Chain for the AI World
    Product: Adobe Experience Manager, Adobe Experience Platform, Adobe Experience Cloud, Adobe GenAI

    From Dialog to Data: Turning Conversations into Business Impact [S107]

    , Principal Product Manager, Digital Experience, Adobe

    , Group Manager, Insights & Analytics, Adobe

    Discover how analytics for conversational agents helps brands optimize dialog flows, analyze sentiment, identify journey drop-off points, and attribute business outcomes to conversational channels. Learn best practices from Adobe’s own implementation of these capabilities. Explore practical strategies for creating operational dashboards, extracting behavioral signals, and activating key audiences to deliver personalized experiences and improve business performance. See what’s next for conversational analytics and how you can embed these capabilities to accelerate digital transformation.

    Key takeaways:

    • Learn to surface engagement, sentiment, and conversion metrics from dialog
    • Get practical methods to blend conversation analytics with web, app, and customer data for unified journey mapping and activation
    • Explore how Adobe implemented conversational analytics internally, covering architecture, data pipelines, governance, dashboards, and the KPIs that demonstrate measurable ROI and business impact at scale
    Track: Analytics
    Product: Adobe Analytics, Adobe Customer Journey Analytics

    4:00 p.m. Monday, Apr 20
    Monday, Apr 20
    From Legacy to Lift-Off: How Esri Scaled B2B Marketing [S746]

    B2B marketers are under pressure to deliver personalization that drives engagement, pipeline, and measurable ROI, yet many remain constrained by legacy marketing automation, fragmented data, and rigid Martech stacks. Platform migration alone might not be enough — real lift-off happens when data, activation, and measurement work together across the B2B funnel. Join PwC, Esri, and Adobe to explore a real-world B2B transformation where a global enterprise modernized its Martech ecosystem, migrating from Marketing Cloud Engagement to Adobe Marketo, integrating Adobe Experience Platform as a unifying data layer, and unlocking actionable customer insights and scalable activation across marketing and sales. Learn how Esri connected customer data, orchestration, and activation to support advanced segmentation, omnichannel engagement, and a clear roadmap toward journey orchestration and AI-enabled personalization.

    Key takeaways:

    • A modernization blueprint from Marketing Cloud Engagement to Marketo, with Adobe Real-Time Customer Data Platform supporting scale and activation
    • How unified data and Martech integration helps drive operational potential and marketing ROI
    • The right metrics to demonstrate impact before downstream revenue is realized

    By clicking add to schedule, I agree the Adobe family of companies may share my contact information with PwC for marketing purposes.

    Track: Customer Data Management and Collaboration, Analytics, B2B Customer Journeys
    Product: Adobe Experience Platform, Adobe Marketo Engage, Adobe Real-Time CDP, Adobe Journey Optimizer B2B Edition, Adobe Real-Time CDP B2B Edition, Adobe Journey Optimizer, Adobe Customer Journey Analytics B2B Edition

    Tuesday, Apr 21
    From Outputs to Outcomes: Rewiring Marketing Teams for Performance [CP10]

    , Global Chief Strategy Officer, Omnicom (RAPP)

    Marketing teams are stuck in static, calendar-based campaign cycles while customers live in always-on, real-time worlds. The result: missed opportunities, slower decisions, and teams optimizing for outputs instead of outcomes. Learn how to rewire your marketing around continuous customer experience improvement using Adobe Experience Cloud. We'll demonstrate how a connected customer journey hub, unified with Adobe Workfront, Real-Time CDP, and Adobe Journey Optimizer, enables marketing teams to operate like high-performing product teams, surfacing opportunities, prioritizing what matters, and activating at scale without sacrificing brand integrity.

    Key takeaways:

    • Move from annual planning cycles to continuous activation where customer insights fuel personalized marketing journeys and cross-channel campaigns
    • Automate orchestration from insight to action
    • Build a repeatable framework that connects every marketing initiative to the metrics that matter, growing customer value and loyalty, not just optimizing single campaigns

    By clicking add to schedule, I agree the Adobe family of companies may share my contact information with Omnicom for marketing purposes.

    Track: Customer Data Management and Collaboration, B2B Customer Journeys, Content Supply Chain for the AI World
    Product: Adobe Experience Platform, Adobe Workfront, Adobe Real-Time CDP, Adobe Journey Optimizer

    1:00 p.m. Tuesday, Apr 21
    Tuesday, Apr 21
    From Personalization to Connected Fandom: Bridging Physical and Digital with AI [S716]

    , CEO/Co-Founder, Lava.ai

    , CTO Americas, WPP

    , Vice President, Marketing Technology and Consumer Products, NFL

    Last year, sports leaders set a bold ambition: grow fandom and engage fans at the speed of need with unmatched personalization. That vision is now reality.

    Today, the next frontier is clear — connecting physical and digital experiences through the power of AI.

    Join leaders from the NFL, Lava and VML as they share how modern sports organizations are evolving from real-time personalization to fully connected fan ecosystems. From stadium to second screen, from tentpole moments to always-on engagement, learn how AI, unified fan data, and operational alignment are enabling leagues to deliver seamless, contextual experiences across every touchpoint.

    This session explores how to:

    • Move beyond isolated campaigns to orchestrated, AI-driven fan journeys
    • Bridge in-venue and digital engagement into one connected experience
    • Build the data and operating model required to activate at “game speed”
    • Turn real-time moments into long-term fan value

    Discover how connected experiences are redefining fan engagement — and why AI is the engine powering the future of sports.

    Key Takeaways

    • How leading leagues evolved from real-time personalization to fully connected fan ecosystems
    • What it takes to unify physical and digital touchpoints using AI
    • The operating model required to deliver engagement at “game speed”
    • How connected experiences drive measurable growth in fandom, ticketing, and lifetime value

    By clicking add to schedule, I agree the Adobe family of companies may share my contact information with VML for marketing purposes.

    Track: Customer Engagement
    Product: Adobe Analytics, Adobe Target, Adobe Experience Platform, Adobe Experience Cloud, Adobe Real-Time CDP, Adobe Customer Journey Analytics, Adobe Journey Optimizer

    , Director of Portfolio Strategy, Firefly Enterprise Solutions, Adobe

    , CVP, Marketing Technology, Microsoft

    , Vice President, Marketing Technology and Consumer Products, NFL

    Enterprises are moving past pilots and moving up the maturity curve in scaling content production with control, quality, and measurable ROI.     From Pilots to Platforms: Scaling Secure, Trusted AI for Enterprise ROI [S852]

    , SVP, Chief Security Officer, Adobe

    , Director of Product Management, Adobe

    Enterprise AI success is defined by security posture, operational trust, regulatory readiness, and sustained business impact. As AI becomes embedded into critical workflows, organizations must design for identity protection, data security, auditability, human oversight, and resilience — not as afterthoughts but as core platform capabilities. At the same time, organizations are modernizing Centers of Excellence into Centers of Intelligence that align security, data, legal, IT, and business teams around a shared operating model for responsible AI adoption.

    Key takeaways:

    • Explore how secure-by-design AI platforms enable faster deployment, higher adoption, lower operational risk, and stronger ROI
    • Practical frameworks for embedding security and governance without slowing innovation and turning trust into a strategic advantage
    Track: AI, Innovation, and Leadership Trends, Orchestrating Experiences with AI Agents
    Product: Adobe Experience Manager Forms, Adobe GenAI

    From Silos to Synergy with a Connected Intelligent Engagement Ecosystem [S650]

    , Group Manager, Product Marketing, Adobe

    , Head of Customer Engagement Campaign Activation, General Motors

    Customer expectations for personalized, seamless experiences have never been higher, and brands that fail to deliver risk losing relevance fast. And with marketing budgets under intense scrutiny, efficiency and ROI are critical. To meet these challenges, organizations must move beyond fragmented processes and embrace connected, interoperable technologies infused with AI capabilities to unlock elevated workflows and unified customer experiences. Explore how this connected approach enables real-time, omnichannel engagement, hyper-personalization at scale, and accelerated time to value — all while maintaining governance and trust to ensure sustainable success.

    Key takeaways:

    • Strategies to leverage innovative, interconnected technologies for continuous intelligence and optimized customer engagements
    • How to enrich customer experiences and drive revenue growth with data, AI, and advanced optimization techniques

    From Spreadsheets to AI: H&R Block Transforms Marketing with Mix Modeler [S106]

    , Sr. Product Manager, Adobe

    , Director of Marketing Data, Analytics and Technology, H&R Block

    Marketing moves fast, so your measurement should too. Join H&R Block to hear how they replaced manual spreadsheets and traditional marketing mix modeling with an AI-powered measurement engine built on Adobe Mix Modeler. Ahead of the 2026 tax season, the team reimagined how they plan, measure, and optimize marketing performance. Get a behind-the-scenes look at how H&R Block uses AI-driven insights to make faster investment decisions, identify channel synergies, and optimize campaigns while they’re live — not months later. Learn how they moved from slow, retrospective analysis to real-time insights that improve efficiency, unlock cost savings, and help marketers move faster with confidence.

    Key takeaways: 

    • How H&R Block operationalized AI-driven marketing measurement with Adobe Mix Modeler
    • Lessons from moving beyond spreadsheets and traditional MMM
    • How channel synergies and real-time insights drive smarter planning and better ROI
    Track: Analytics
    Product: Adobe Mix Modeler, Adobe Customer Journey Analytics

      (Wednesday, Apr 223:30 AM - 4:30 AM IST)

    From Touchpoint to Dialog: Account-Based Marketing Gets a Face [OS756]

    , Kaltura

    Marketing teams have mastered page creation, personalization rules, and ABM landing pages, yet many digital experiences are still optimized for delivery rather than engagement. Explore how agentic avatars and generative UI introduce real-time, adaptive guidance inside digital experiences and how these capabilities come together within Adobe Experience Manager to extend sites beyond static content delivery. See how agentic avatars and gen UI orchestrate content, video, and conversational AI to adapt messaging and guidance based on user intent, role, and behavior without rebuilding pages or fragmenting campaigns. Key takeaways:

    • Practical patterns for designing adaptive, guided content and account-based marketing experiences
    • Ways to orchestrate content, video, and interaction through a single experience layer
    • Organizational and technical considerations for evolving content sites beyond static delivery
    Track: Orchestrating Experiences with AI Agents
    Product: Adobe Experience Manager, Adobe Marketo Engage, Adobe Marketo Measure

    Future of Marketing Automation: B2B Customer Journeys Vision and Roadmap [S201]

    , Sr. Director, Product Marketing, B2B, Adobe

    , Director of Product, B2B Customer Journeys, Adobe

    Explore the next chapter of B2B marketing innovation with Adobe Marketo Engage and Adobe Journey Optimizer B2B Edition product leaders. Adobe is transforming revenue marketing through agentic AI, real-time intelligence, and unified data activation. Agentic journeys and AI assistants empower marketers to design, orchestrate, and optimize buying-group experiences with greater precision and speed. New gen AI content tools streamline creation and testing across campaigns and enhance analytics and partner integrations.

    Michael Ahearn
    Wednesday, Apr 22
    9:00 a.m. Wednesday, Apr 22
    Future-Proofing Customer Engagement Through Messaging [S718]

    , North America GTM Lead, RCS, Google

    , VP Customer Development & Strategy, Sinch

    The way companies leverage mobile messaging has evolved from basic alerts into being a critical pillar of modern customer engagement. Driven by expectations for immediacy, personalization and rich experiences, the mobile channels have evolved drastically, and brands must rethink how mobile messaging fits within the broader customer journey.

    In this session, Adobe ISV Platinum Partner, Sinch, a world leader in mobile messaging - and Google, the global driver of RCS , will showcase how you can leverage traditional SMS and the rapidly growing RCS channel to create immersive, secure and immediate mobile experience that cut through the noise and open for new types of customer engagement.

    Attendees will further learn how to connect mobile messaging with real-time customer signals, orchestration cross channel journeys and deliver personalized experiences at scale while maintaining global compliance and reliability. We'll also examine how the emerging conversational AI-driven messaging interactions open for new use cases for mobile experiences.

    Walk away with a clear understanding of how to design a flexible, future ready messaging strategy built for what's next.

    Key Takeaways:

    • Understand how mobile messaging has evolved into a core engagement channel
    • Learn how to orchestrate mobile messaging within cross-channel customer journeys
    • Discover how RCS enables richer branded customer experiences
    • See how real-time data and personalization transform messaging into a true engagement channel
    • Gain a framework for building a flexible messaging strategy

    By clicking add to schedule, I agree the Adobe family of companies may share my contact information with Sinch for marketing purposes.

    Track: Customer Engagement, AI, Innovation, and Leadership Trends
    Product: Adobe Journey Optimizer

    Tuesday, Apr 21
    Get Meta with Your Data: Shaping the Future of Your Marketing Ecosystem [OS808]

    , Director Product Management, Workfront Planning, Adobe

    , Principal Product Marketing Manager, Adobe

    Metadata acts as the context engine of your marketing ecosystem, giving structure, meaning, and connectedness to every campaign, asset, and activity. It enables teams to align work to business priorities, generate consistent insights, and activate content and campaigns with clarity and scale. Metadata is also critical in the development of an AI‑enabled ecosystem, where structured data becomes the fuel for contextual agent activation. Learn how to design a scalable taxonomy and metadata framework using Adobe Workfront Planning. Get best practices for marketing taxonomy, how to align metadata to strategic goals, and building a foundation that powers intelligent decision‑making.
    Key takeaways:

    • Design a scalable marketing taxonomy that creates consistent structure and strengthens cross‑functional alignment
    • Increase operational clarity by applying metadata practices that support better organization and visibility
    • Build a metadata foundation for an AI‑enabled ecosystem, where structured data fuels contextual agent activation and smarter decision‑making
    Track: Workflow and Planning
    Product: Adobe Workfront

    Tuesday, Apr 21
    11:00 a.m. Tuesday, Apr 21
    Globalization: No Home Field Advantage – Building Customers and Fanbases from Scratch [S150]

    , Chief Growth Officer, Williams F1 Team

    , Director of Digital Strategy, Real Madrid

    Global reach is easy. Cultural trust isn’t. As markets become saturated, the next decade of growth for every major brand runs through expansion to new cities and countries — a true blue-sky opportunity if done right.
    This panel, developed in collaboration with Sport Beach, brings together perspectives from global leagues, teams, and athletes to explore how brands can understand cultural nuance and deliver content and moments that resonate in real time.

    How do you move a fan from passive follower to active supporter? And how do you scale that connection across markets? Personalization will power this shift. Hear how leading sports organizations are building loyal fanbases beyond their home markets by creating experiences that feel locally relevant while staying true to their global identity. Take a deep dive into how to build cultural trust that turns a foreign property into a local institution.

    Key takeaways:

    • Explore what every brand can steal from the sports playbook when it comes to engaging fans on a global scale
    • Learn how sports franchises diagnose whether a new market has genuine conversion potential and apply that discipline to your own global expansion strategy
    Track: Customer Data Management and Collaboration, Customer Engagement
    Product: Not Product-Specific
    • Tuesday, Apr 2111:30 AM - 12:30 PM PDT
      (Wednesday, Apr 2212:00 AM - 1:00 AM IST)

    Growing GMV in the agentic AI era: The Adobe Commerce Roadmap [S301]

    , Sr. Director, Product Marketing and Product Management, Adobe Commerce, Adobe

    , Sr. Solutions Consultant, Adobe

    , Director, Data & Digital Technology, Nestlé Professional Solutions

    Agentic AI is transforming commerce. LLM-powered channels and conversational assistants are reshaping how products are discovered, evaluated, and ultimately chosen.

    Join this session to learn how to capitalize on this generational shift to boost product discoverability, deliver personalized and engaging shopping journeys, and drive GMV growth. You’ll also hear from Nestlé on how they are modernizing their commerce stack on Adobe Commerce to accelerate innovation, scale operations,  and drive business impact.

    Learn how to:
    • Optimize product discoverability across layered queries on LLMs
    •Increase conversion on your storefronts through AI-driven conversational shopping assistants.
    • Deliver rich personalized experiences through AI-driven content authoring
    • Speed-up expansion with a highly flexible catalog that supports multiple channels and channel-based pricing
    • Streamline customization and adoption of our new SaaS platform with AI-assisted migration and development tools

    Track: Commerce
    Product: Adobe Commerce

    11:00 a.m. Monday, Apr 20
    Monday, Apr 20
    How Adobe AI Drives Speed to Insight [S109]

    , Managing Principal, Expert Solution Consultant, Adobe

    , Sr. Manager, Digital and Customer Analytics, Lenovo

    AI agents are changing how organizations access insights, but they’re only as effective as the data they can reach. To truly democratize analytics, agents need direct access to granular, customer-level data, not just pre-aggregated summarizations. Adobe Customer Journey Analytics solves this by allowing agents to query complete datasets on the fly, enabling them to answer new, unscripted business questions that go beyond predefined paths. Lokesh Alluri shares how Lenovo’s business users are self-serving insights at scale within an enterprise-wide AI ecosystem. Lenovo’s journey highlights how to achieve trusted data interpretation without disrupting existing technology investments. Learn about new capabilities that improve LLM efficiencies and unlock new use cases, including enabling integration with Customer Journey Analytics. We’ll outline Adobe’s strategy for bridging CMO and CIO needs by extending analysis beyond Customer Journey Analytics interfaces. Learn new capabilities that improve LLM efficiencies and unlock new use cases, including accessing Customer Journey Analytics insights in Microsoft Copilot via the Adobe Marketing Agent.
    Key takeaways:

    • Unlock the power of granular, customer-level data to fuel flexible AI agent responses
    • Hear how Lenovo empowers business users with trusted, self-service insights at scale
    • Explore how MCP Server connects Customer Journey Analytics insights to your wider data ecosystem
    Track: Analytics
    Product: Adobe Customer Journey Analytics
    • Monday, Apr 2011:30 AM - 12:30 PM PDT
      (Tuesday, Apr 2112:00 AM - 1:00 AM IST)

    Monday, Apr 20
    How Adobe.com Wins in an Agentic World [S326]

    , Principal Product Marketing Manager, Adobe

    , Sr. Director, Adobe.com for Business, Adobe

    Buyers now ask AI to shortlist vendors, and many brands just don't show up. Meanwhile, content is everywhere, but hard to find, hard to tailor to buying groups, and hard to reuse after the deal. Learn the simple system we use on Adobe.com to make our content easy for AI to cite, easy for teams to reuse, and relevant for every role in buying groups, so that you can drive more pipeline, shorten cycles, and accelerate adoption.

    Key takeaways:

    • How to make B2B web content easy for AI to understand, trust, and cite, so that you show up earlier in buying journeys
    • How to find content effectively and create account-specific experiences to make deals move faster
    • How to reuse the same content framework for onboarding to speed up adoption and expansion
    Track: Brand Visibility and Content Management in the Agentic Web
    Product: Adobe Experience Manager, Adobe Experience Manager Sites, Adobe Experience Manager Assets, Adobe Experience Manager Sites Optimizer, Adobe Learning Manager, Adobe LLM Optimizer

    General audience

    11:00 a.m. Monday, Apr 20
    Monday, Apr 20
    How Adobe Reimagined B2B Paid Media with Adobe Journey Optimizer B2B Edition [OS217]

    , Director, Campaign Capabilities - Digital Experience, Adobe

    , Principal Product Manager, CXO Data & Technology COE, Adobe

    Paid media is one of the most expensive and operationally complex channels in B2B marketing. Fragmented audiences, manual list management, and limited visibility into account progression make it difficult for marketers to prioritize spend, manage overlap intentionally, and align paid media with broader revenue goals. 

    Learn how Adobe applied Adobe Journey Optimizer B2B Edition and Adobe Experience Platform to rethink paid media activation through orchestration. Through a real Adobe-on-Adobe implementation, see how signal-driven, account-aware orchestration connects data, journeys, and activation in practice, creating a single source of truth for paid media decisioning and enabling more efficient execution at scale. 

    Key takeaways 

    • Why paid media breaks down at scale and where traditional activation models fall short 
    • How account-level orchestration enables smarter prioritization and intentional overlap management
    • How Adobe built a Paid Media Controller using account-level journeys to prioritize programs, intentionally manage overlap, and activate audiences directly to paid media channels with no manual handoffs
    Track: B2B Customer Journeys
    Product: Adobe Experience Platform, Adobe Journey Optimizer B2B Edition
    • Monday, Apr 2011:15 AM - 11:30 AM PDT
      (11:45 PM - 12:00 AM IST)

    Include in embedded catalog
    Mario Ciabarra
    Tuesday, Apr 21
    3:00 p.m. Tuesday, Apr 21
    How Agentic AI Answers Mission-Critical CX Questions While You Sleep [S721]

    , Founder & CEO, Quantum Metric

    , Chief Product Officer, Quantum Metric

    Digital teams don’t lack data. They lack time and shared understanding. Across every organization, a small set of critical customer experience questions determine conversion, retention, and trust. The problem isn’t knowing what to measure—it’s how long it takes to understand why something changed and what to do next.

    Agentic AI changes that model. Instead of relying on dashboards, static alerts, and manual investigation, agentic systems work continuously in the background. They monitor key journeys, detect meaningful change, investigate root causes, and quantify business impact automatically. While teams sleep, or focus on building experiences, agents analyze thousands of behavioral signals across journeys to surface clear, contextual answers grounded in real customer behavior. The result is faster alignment, fewer blind spots, and a shorter path from signal to decision.

    In this session, explore how agentic analytics moves beyond reactive reporting to autonomous understanding, complementing existing analytics investments by collapsing the distance between insight and action. Key takeaways:

    • How teams define agentic systems around their most important CX outcomes—so answers surface automatically, not after weeks of investigation
    • How agentic AI reduces analysis bottlenecks by continuously detecting, explaining, and quantifying experience changes across journeys
    • How autonomous analytics complements existing Adobe and analytics stacks by accelerating decision-making without replacing core tools

    By clicking add to schedule, I agree the Adobe family of companies may share my contact information with Quantum Metric for marketing purposes.

    Track: Analytics, AI, Innovation, and Leadership Trends, Orchestrating Experiences with AI Agents
    • Tuesday, Apr 213:00 PM - 4:00 PM PDT

    How AstraZeneca is Transforming its Content Supply Chain with Adobe [S405]

    , Sr. Product Marketing Manager, Adobe

    , Executive Director, Commercial Excellence and Digital Marketing Technologies, AstraZeneca

    Is your marketing team under increasing pressure to produce more content, support more variations, and move at greater and greater speed — all while proving measurable impact. Hear how customers are using Adobe GenStudio to transform campaign production and drive business results. Discover how teams are scaling digital asset creation, automating high-volume campaign workflows, and accelerating speed to market without sacrificing brand consistency. See how generative AI and connected workflows come together to reduce manual effort, streamline approvals, and enable regionally relevant and personalized content at scale. 

    Beyond the technology, this session focuses on outcomes - how operationalizing GenStudio drives efficiency across creative and marketing teams, increases campaign velocity, and delivers measurable ROI. 

    Key takeaways: 

    • How an enterprise team scaled high-volume campaign production using Adobe GenStudio 
    • How automation and gen AI reduce manual design work and accelerate turnaround times 
    • How connected workflows improve speed, consistency, and collaboration
    Track: Content Supply Chain for the AI World
    Product: Adobe GenStudio

    How Collaboration Turns Vision into Business-Ready Content [S418]

    , Director, Solutions Enablement, Adobe

    , Director, Eyeful Presentations

    Get a behind-the-scenes look at how Adobe’s marketing teams partner with our creative agency, Eyeful Presentation, to bring ideas to life. We’ll showcase the collaborative process — from shaping the vision to refining the final presentations, demos, and content that power customer conversations. See how close partnership, clear storytelling, and consistent visual design come together to deliver content that is both beautiful and effective. Learn how Eyeful uses Adobe tools, like Creative Cloud, Frame.io, and Acrobat Studio, to build, iterate, and scale customer-facing assets to drive real business impact.

    Key takeaways:

    • Great content is built through close collaboration, not handoffs
    • Adobe’s Creative Cloud and Acrobat tools turn ideas into business-ready assets
    • Consistency in story and design drives customer trust and business results

    How CrowdStrike is Transforming its B2B Engagement through AJO B2B Edition [S211]

    , Sr. Product Manager, Adobe

    , Director, Marketing Operations, CrowdStrike

    , Sr. Director, Marketing IT, CrowdStrike

    Learn how CrowdStrike transformed 500 strategic cold accounts into engaged leads using precision-targeted outreach and journey orchestration with Adobe Journey Optimizer B2B Edition. CrowdStrike’s pragmatic implementation leverages existing Marketo profiles while building a continuous feedback loop for personalization throughout the customer lifecycle. The hybrid journey architecture combines scheduled campaigns with trigger-based experiences to deliver personalized content addressing specific cybersecurity challenges. With 60% growth in marketability to decision-makers and 30% improvement in campaign engagement, CrowdStrike’s implementation demonstrates the power of unified content delivery and automated data-driven journeys. 

    Key takeaways:

    • Implement a phased integration approach that quickly delivers results while building toward a robust end-to-end architecture 
    • Overcome siloed customer data to enable full buying group engagement through targeted journey orchestration 
    • Reduce acquisition costs and accelerate pipeline velocity by combining paid media with personalized content journeys 
    Track: B2B Customer Journeys
    Product: Adobe Marketo Engage, Adobe Journey Optimizer B2B Edition

    Monday, Apr 20
    How Customers Scale Real Workflows with Agentic AI from Intent to Action [OS810]

    , Senior AI Product Manager, Adobe

    , Sr. Marketing Lead - Personalization, Sling TV

    What happens when the work that used to take your team weeks takes minutes instead? In this customer-led session, Sling TV shares how it uses Adobe Experience Platform AI Assistant to plan, execute, and scale real marketing workflows, from analyzing customer behavior to building audiences to launching personalized journeys. Hear directly from a marketing lead about what changes when AI removes the waiting, the manual handoffs, and the tool-switching that slows teams down and what that frees them up to do instead.

    Key takeaways:

    • How a marketing team went from business question to live campaign in minutes using natural language across data, audiences, and journeys without writing a single query or switching tools
    • What shifts for the people doing the work when AI handles the coordination, and how that changes the way teams think, decide, and operate at scale
    Track: Orchestrating Experiences with AI Agents
    Product: Adobe Experience Platform Agent Orchestrator

    How Fanatics Leveled Up Its Email Game with Adobe Journey Optimizer [S526]

    , Sr. Manager, CJM Solutions Consulting, Adobe

    , VP, Product Management, Fanatics

    Fanatics and Adobe explore the crawl-walk-sprint evolution that transformed Fanatics’ email program with Adobe Journey Optimizer. Learn how Fanatics built a flexible data foundation that allows marketers to move fast and iterate without complex segmentation schemes, enabling real-time personalization at massive scale. Hear how the company increased relevance with fewer sends, accelerated speed-to-send during high-stakes moments like the World Series, and expanded customer journeys to include SMS alongside email. Explore how Fanatics manages arbitration and prioritization across brands, ensuring that fans receive the right message at the right time.

    Key takeaways:

    • Fanatics’ journey to the latest innovations in email, AI-assisted content creation and quality checks, experimentation, and cross-channel orchestration
    • A practical playbook for delivering smarter, more personalized engagement without sacrificing speed or control
    Track: Customer Engagement

    , SVP, US Operations, Publicis Groupe

    , Program Director, Publicis

    Creative agencies face an increasingly competitive landscape where success depends on delivering standout creative faster while clearly demonstrating ROI for their clients. Dive into how Publicis evolved its operating model to balance creative excellence with business performance.     Tracey Craft
    4:00 p.m. Monday, Apr 20
    Monday, Apr 20
    How SAP Is Rebuilding the Content Supply Chain for AI-Powered Scale [S707]

    , VP of Content Marketing, SAP

    , Managing Director, Accenture

    SAP, in partnership with Accenture, is modernizing its global content supply chain to set a new industry standard for speed, scale, and personalization, powered by modular workflows, governed content, and an integrated technology ecosystem. Tracey Craft from SAP, and Allison Borden from Accenture, share how SAP is transforming end-to-end content strategy, creation, governance, and activation using Adobe Workfront, Adobe Experience Manager, Adobe Experience Platform, and Marketo. Sponsored by SAP’s CMO and spanning marketing and IT teams across the US, Europe, and APAC, this initiative demonstrates how large enterprises can centrally govern content, automate workflows, and activate data-driven experiences at scale without sacrificing regional flexibility or brand integrity.

    Key takeaways:

    • Get real-world insights from SAP and Accenture into building a scalable, AI-ready content supply chain
    • Moving from strategy to execution across a complex global organization

    By clicking add to schedule, I agree the Adobe family of companies may share my contact information with Accenture for marketing purposes.

    Track: Content Supply Chain for the AI World
    Product: Adobe Experience Platform, Adobe Experience Cloud, Adobe Workfront, Adobe Marketo Engage, Adobe Experience Manager Assets

    Include in embedded catalog
    1:00 p.m. Tuesday, Apr 21
    Tuesday, Apr 21
    How the NFL Shares Content Without Giving Away the Playbook [S923]

    , Sr. DAM Strategist, Adobe

    , Sr. Product Manager, NFL

    NFL Enterprises and Adobe—an Official Partner of the NFL—have partnered to deliver an AI-powered content supply chain designed to help the league and all 32 clubs scale fan experience personalized engagement across digital channels. Achieving this vision requires a content foundation that enables collaboration without sacrificing independence or control.

     

    In this session, learn how the NFL worked with Adobe Support & Services to centralize digital asset management using Adobe Experience Manager (AEM) as a core pillar of its content ecosystem. By bringing the league and all 32 clubs onto a single AEM instance, the teams enabled dynamic permissions, automated metadata and taxonomy, and controlled asset sharing across clubs.

     

    Attendees will see how this approach reduces manual effort, accelerates content delivery, and supports AI-enabled workflows while maintaining strong governance—lessons that apply to any organization managing content across teams or business units.

    Key Takeaways:
    •    How to design a centralized DAM that supports dynamic, granular permissions at scale
    •    Strategies for automating metadata and taxonomy to reduce manual effort
    •    Best practices for enabling secure content sharing without duplication

    Track: Brand Visibility and Content Management in the Agentic Web
    Product: Adobe Workfront, Adobe Experience Manager Assets

      (Wednesday, Apr 222:00 AM - 3:00 AM IST)

    How Vanguard Reimagined Marketing with GenStudio for Performance Marketing [S406]

    , Director, Product Marketing, Digital Experience, Adobe

    , Sr. Group Creative Director: Technical Integration and Creative Operations, Vanguard

    , Head of Products for Adobe GenStudio, Adobe

    Vanguard is reimagining how creative and marketing teams work by embedding AI into content production and campaign optimization. Susan McLaughlin, Senior Creative Group Director for Technology Integration and Creative Operations at Vanguard, is joined by Adobe GenStudio leaders Gagan Mand, Head of Product Marketing, and Alok Ojha, Head of Product. Together they share how roles, workflows, and tools were redesigned so creatives can focus on big ideas while marketers drive performance with AI.

    See how Vanguard’s early adoption of Adobe Firefly laid the foundation for its use of Adobe GenStudio for Performance Marketing to generate personalized campaign content, automate repeatable work, and learn from campaign performance over time. Get a preview of how Vanguard is leveraging Adobe’s newest GenStudio module, GenStudio for Content Marketing, to maximize the ROI of long-form content with AI.

    Key takeaways:

    • How Vanguard embeds AI into creative and marketing workflows with Adobe Firefly and GenStudio
    • How to redesign roles, automate content, personalize campaigns, and turn performance insights into higher ROI across channels 
    Track: Content Supply Chain for the AI World

    4:00 p.m. Tuesday, Apr 21
    Tuesday, Apr 21
    How Workday Drives Modern ABM with Adobe and Bombora [OS761]

    For too long, an account-based marketing strategy has been synonymous with heavy investments in siloed software platforms. Those days are over. Welcome to an open, flexible approach to ABM, powered by Bombora + Adobe. Hear how Workday has transformed its Adobe B2B Real-Time CDP into a high-performance engine that flexibly delivers ABM orchestration when they need it and where they need it. Leverage the full spectrum of first- and third party data in real time to drive account segmentation. Turn any Adobe CDP segment into at-scale, addressable audiences for the open web, CTV, and social platforms like Reddit with Bombora’s audience activation.

    Key takeaways:

    • How to harness seamless data flows to ensure that as your account segments update, your media activation easily follows
    • Maintaining constant alignment between your account intelligence strategy and execution
    Track: Customer Data Management and Collaboration, B2B Customer Journeys, Customer Engagement
    Product: Adobe Real-Time CDP, Adobe Real-Time CDP Collaboration

    Intermediate
    Include in embedded catalog
    Wednesday, Apr 22
    9:00 a.m. Wednesday, Apr 22
    How Wyndham Uses AI Content Intelligence to Drive Conversion [S104]

    , Product Manager, Adobe Analytics, Adobe

    , Senior Analyst, Digital Marketing Analytics, Wyndham Hotels & Resorts

    Why do certain creative elements outperform others across different channels? Do snowy landscapes boost CTR on web while ocean scenes convert better in paid social? Does social proof win first-time buyers on social while scarcity drives repeat purchases in mobile apps? For the first time, you can answer these questions with precision. Adobe Content Analytics now brings AI-powered content intelligence to native mobile apps and paid media, breaking down every asset into its DNA — colors, scenes, objects, and more. Uncover how the same creative performs differently across channels. Learn how to attribute revenue to individual assets, identify granular traits that drive conversions for specific segments, and close the loop on your content supply chain for maximum ROI. Hear from Katie Makin on how Wyndham leverages Content Analytics to understand what drives conversion across Mobile and Web channels, and optimizes creative for measurable impact. 

    Key takeaways:

    • Pinpoint which content attributes convert best on mobile vs. web vs. paid media
    • Attribute revenue impact to individual assets across your digital ecosystem
    • Optimize creative supply chains by knowing what resonates where
    Track: Analytics
    Product: Adobe Content Analytics

    IBM Drives Technical Content at Scale with Adobe Experience Manager Guides [OS340]

    , Principal Technical Architect with Adobe Customer Solutions for AEM Guides and DITA, Adobe

    , Chief Architect for IBM's Content Ecosystem, IBM

    With 40 years of technical content, thousands of products, 20 million plus pages of content, and more than 1,000 content creators, IBM has built one of the world’s most extensive information ecosystems. To take this global operation to the next level, IBM launched a bold initiative to create a unified, intelligent content foundation — powered by Adobe Experience Manager Guides.

    In this session, Kerry Landis, Executive Architect for the Adobe Platform at IBM, and Chad Dybdahl, Senior Technical Architect, Adobe Experience Manager Guides at Adobe, share how IBM is modernizing content operations at unprecedented scale. Discover how a shared vision, enterprise-wide collaboration, and smart technology come together to enhance author productivity, accelerate publishing, and unlock new possibilities for AI-driven content experiences. Learn how IBM’s forward-looking approach enables faster time to market, stronger governance, and a customer-centric content experience strategy.

    Key takeaways:

    • How IBM scales global content operations with AEM Guides across thousands of products
    • The measurable impact of AEM Guides on content velocity, quality, and governance
    • How IBM’s forward-looking strategy lays the groundwork for intelligent content operations
    Track: Brand Visibility and Content Management in the Agentic Web
    Product: Adobe Experience Manager Guides
    • Tuesday, Apr 214:30 PM - 4:45 PM PDT
      (Wednesday, Apr 225:00 AM - 5:15 AM IST)

    Identify Pipeline Friction with Adobe Customer Journey Analytics B2B [L120]

    , Solution Consultant - Customer Journey Analytics, Adobe

    , Practice Lead, Data & Insights Technical Validation, Adobe

    Identify where deals slow down, which personas drive momentum, and how to turn those insights into action. Learn how Adobe Customer Journey Analytics B2B Edition can help you pinpoint true bottlenecks in your funnel. Analyze how engagement from specific buying groups, titles, and functions influences sales velocity, and discover which types of content resonate most with each audience to strengthen engagement. Learn how to build a high-value audience and publish from Customer Journey Analytics into Adobe Experience Platform.

    Key takeaways:

    • Learn how to use data to identify where opportunities slow down by segments, such as industry, region, or product
    • Identify titles that are most engaged by stage and which personas are most associated with deal progression by stage
    • Get a repeatable workflow to diagnose friction, quantify persona impact, and operationalize insights for measurable action

    3:00 p.m. Tuesday, Apr 21
    Indie to Blockbuster: Customer Experience Use Cases That Ship Value Faster [S651]

    , Director, Product Management, Adobe

    , Sr. Director, CRM & Personalization (Marketing), Ulta Beauty

    , Sr. Director - MarTech, Ulta Beauty

    , Sr. Director, Product Management, Ulta Beauty

    Join us to discover a proven use‑case framework — Who/What/Where/Why/How — to translate customer experience ambitions into measurable business outcomes. We’ll walk through the core model and illustrate the collaborative process from use case ideation, to launch in market, through real-world examples, showing how teams align quickly, prioritize the right work, and prove value with clear test‑and‑control and KPI practices. We’ll also spotlight how agentic capabilities accelerate ideation, orchestration, and measurement across parallel agile workflows.

    You'll learn how the framework scales from quick wins to orchestrated, multi‑channel journeys that drive durable outcomes and leave with facilitator guidance and lightweight templates to run the workshop in your own organization, plus B2C and B2B real‑world examples to make the methods stick back at the office.

    Key takeaways:

    • A reusable use‑case framework and maturity model to evolve from quick wins to orchestrated, multi‑channel journeys
    • A practical playbook for cross‑functional workshops — roles, cadence, ownership, and follow‑ups
    • An understanding of how agentic capabilities speed up ideation, activation, and measurement
    • A simple prioritization method combining business‑impact sizing with feasibility to focus investment where it pays
    • B2C and B2B patterns and best practice callouts to take back to your teams
    Track: Customer Data Management and Collaboration, Analytics, Customer Engagement, Orchestrating Experiences with AI Agents
    Product: Adobe Experience Platform, Adobe Real-Time CDP, Adobe Real-Time CDP Collaboration, Adobe Experience Manager Sites, Adobe Experience Manager Assets, Adobe Mix Modeler, Adobe Content Analytics, Adobe Experience Platform Agent Orchestrator, Adobe Experience Manager Sites Optimizer, Adobe Customer Journey Analytics, Adobe Journey Optimizer, Adobe LLM Optimizer

    Tuesday, Apr 21
    Inside a Frontier Marketing Organization: AI Transformation in Action [OS757]

    , Corporate Vice President, Global Industry Marketing, Microsoft

    The gap between AI experimentation and enterprise-wide transformation isn’t technology — it’s adaptation. While 78% of marketing organizations have piloted AI tools, fewer than 20% have successfully scaled AI across their content supply chain and customer journey operations. Microsoft marketing transformation leaders share how they’re bridging that gap within their own marketing organization and guiding other leading brands, in partnership with Adobe, to become frontier organizations — those that seamlessly integrate AI across the marketing lifecycle to stay ahead of the competition.

    Key takeaways:

    • Learn how pioneering companies move beyond isolated use cases to orchestrate AI-powered experiences that connect data, content, and customer journeys through a single pane of glass
    • Get actionable insights to help you build an adaptive, AI-driven marketing organization where your teams don’t just adopt AI, they continuously evolve with it
    Track: AI, Innovation, and Leadership Trends
    Product: Not Product-Specific

    Inside Delta Air Lines’ Real-Time Personalization Strategy with Decisioning [S921]

    , Senior Mgr - Enterprise Architecture, Adobe

    , Field Engineer, Adobe

    , Sr. Manager, Personalization Tech and Tools, Delta Air Lines

    Discover how Delta Air Lines is redefining personalization by centralizing decision logic in Adobe Journey Optimizer. Acting as a real-time decisioning layer, Journey Optimizer evaluates customer profiles and context to deliver the most relevant offers, content, and messages across channels. Explore how decision rules, constraints, and AI models work together to drive consistent, data-driven experiences while maintaining governance and agility. Learn how to establish an enterprise-wide decisioning framework that aligns marketing, data science, and technology teams. And gain a practical blueprint for operationalizing Journey Optimizer Decisioning to accelerate engagement, conversions, and long-term customer value.

    Key takeaways:

    • Learn how Adobe Journey Optimizer Decisioning unifies rules, data, and AI to select the best experience for each customer
    • Discover frameworks for designing scalable decision strategies across offers, messages, and channels
    • Understand how brands measure, lift, and optimize decisions using AI models within Journey Optimizer
    Track: Customer Engagement
    Product: Adobe Journey Optimizer

    4:00 p.m. Tuesday, Apr 21
    Tuesday, Apr 21
    Inside MLB’s Playbook: Measuring What Really Drives Demand [OS117]

    , Sr. Marketing Analyst, Major League Baseball

    , Sr. Marketing Analyst, Major League Baseball

    , Director, Product Management, Adobe

    Every marketing team wants to believe great campaigns win the game. But in baseball — and in marketing — performance is shaped by more than execution alone. At Major League Baseball, ticket sales move with win percentage, playoff odds, home game schedules, opponent fanbase size, seasonality, and key on-sale moments. The real challenge isn’t just driving demand — it’s understanding what’s influencing it. MLB shares how they use Adobe Mix Modeler to separate marketing impact from “game-day conditions,” measure what truly drives ticket sales, and plan budgets with greater confidence. See how modeling controllable and uncontrollable factors together helps MLB forecast smarter, allocate more effectively, and turn insight into competitive advantage.

    Key takeaways:

    • How MLB measures ticket sales drivers beyond media, from team performance to schedule dynamics
    • Isolating true marketing impact from external factors with Adobe Mix Modeler
    • How MLB uses these insights to improve planning, forecasting, and budget allocation
    Track: Analytics
    Product: Adobe Mix Modeler
    • Tuesday, Apr 214:15 PM - 4:30 PM PDT
      (Wednesday, Apr 224:45 AM - 5:00 AM IST)

    1:00 p.m. Tuesday, Apr 21
    Tuesday, Apr 21
    Inside OpenAI’s Marketing Stack: Applying AI to Scale Smarter [S724]

    , OpenAI

    , Co-Founder and Chief Evangelist, Knak

    As marketing demand increases, teams face a new challenge: scaling output without adding complexity or risk. Join Jeff Canada, head of B2B Marketing Operations at OpenAI, to hear how his Marketing Operations team is applying AI within their own marketing stack to move faster, stay governed, and reduce operational friction. See how AI helps turn unstructured requests into structured work, supports smarter triage and decisioning, and enables self-serve creation within clear guardrails. We’ll walk through a real-world architecture that connects intake, orchestration, and execution, showing how modern teams can shift from manual hustle to sustainable scale. Leave with practical ideas for applying AI to marketing operations today, plus a clear view of what it takes to scale smarter, not just faster.

    Key takeaways:

    • How to use AI to streamline intake, triage requests, and reduce operational friction
    • A practical framework for enabling self-serve execution while maintaining governance and brand control
    • What a scalable AI-powered marketing ops architecture looks like, and how to apply it to your own stack today

    By clicking add to schedule, I agree the Adobe family of companies may share my contact information with Knak for marketing purposes.

    Track: Workflow and Planning, AI, Innovation, and Leadership Trends, Enterprise Productivity for High-Performing Teams

    Insights Beyond the Stars: Galactic Customer Journey Analytics Tips & Tricks [S103]

    , Sr. Product Manager, Adobe

    , Principal Expert Solutions Consultant, Adobe

    Analytics can sometimes feel like rocket science. Between acting as the pilot, engineer, and navigator, the pressure of the mission can feel immense. You don’t have to explore the void alone! Prepare for liftoff as we share intergalactic tips designed to make Customer Journey Analytics feel like a high-speed slingshot around Jupiter. Explore cosmically powerful storytelling capabilities, expand your horizons through data visualizations, and reach insights at faster than light speed. Whether you’re dealing with a cracked hull or a complex data set, we’ll show you how to solve any problem with the Customer Journey Analytics equivalent of duct tape, tube socks, and ingenuity. Explore our out-of-this-world analytics tips and a chance to win exclusive Customer Journey Analytics swag and prizes. 

    Key takeaways:

    • Master innovative, real-time data transformations to clean up your reporting at warp speed
    • Learn how to integrate extra-terrestrial data dimensions that bring a new perspective to your customer journey
    • Use cosmic storytelling capabilities to share discoveries directly within your reports
    Track: Analytics

    Interbank’s Mar-Tech team shares how they are advancing the company’s omnichannel strategy by applying AI-generated content in Adobe Journey Optimizer to dramatically reduce email time to market by 70%. Hear how the team streamlined production, empowered marketers to create personalization-ready content faster, and unlocked a scalable model for customer-driven communications across channels. Interbank walks through the foundational steps, operating model, and strategic considerations that enable them to move from a slow, manual process to a high-velocity system that fuels rapid iteration and more effective, relevant experiences.

    Invisible or Irresistible: Why Elevating B2B Commerce Is Non-Negotiable [S304]

    , Principal Product Marketing Manager, Adobe

    , Sr. Product Manager, Adobe

    , Business Transformation & Customer Excellence Director, Bosch Home Comfort Group

    , Associate Director, IS Lead Business Consultant, Boehringer Ingelheim USA Corporation

    B2B buying is being rewritten in real time. AI agents are fast becoming the new front door for discovery — and soon, they’ll play an active role in purchasing decisions. If your commerce foundation isn’t modern, you won’t just lose speed, you’ll become harder to find, harder to buy from, and easier to replace. Learn how Adobe Commerce equips you to compete across human and AI-driven buying — scaling catalog and pricing, simplifying quote-to-order and reordering, and making it faster and easier for customers to buy. Hear how Boehringer Ingelheim transformed its B2B experience on Adobe Commerce, accelerating its online sales in the U.S. veterinary segment, launching a rapidly scaling loyalty program, and deploying a global platform with flexible, country-level instances.

    Key takeaways:

    • Lessons learned from modernizing
    • What it takes to modernize, along with a practical modernization blueprint
    • How Adobe Commerce powers the next generation of B2B excellence 
    Track: Commerce
    Product: Adobe Commerce, Adobe Commerce Optimizer

    Leading Through Creativity: The C-Suite as Change Maker [S714]

    , CEO, Code and Theory

    Most transformation strategies fail because they’re built to respond to change that’s already happened. By the time you’ve analyzed the disruption, your competitors have moved on. The highest performing leadership teams don’t transform — they create the conditions that force everyone else to. Explore why speed to imagination matters more than speed to market. Get a framework for turning executive vision into market-shaping action, not through better planning, but through better creative infrastructure.

    Key takeaways:

    • What separates reactive organizations from generative ones
    • How to build creative capacity at the executive level
    • The operational shifts that let leadership teams move from approving ideas to originating them

    By clicking add to schedule, I agree the Adobe family of companies may share my contact information with Code and Theory for marketing purposes.

    Learn from Every Run, Check, and Alert with Key Run and Operate Tooling [L620]

    , Sr. Product Manager, Adobe

    , Director, Solution Architect, Adobe

    Struggling with operational complexity, alert fatigue, or limited end‑to‑end visibility across your Adobe Experience Platform environment? See more, act faster, and optimize continuously by turning every run, check, and alert into actionable insight. Explore core Run and Operate tooling that turns Adobe Experience Platform into your operational command center. Through guided exercises on Job Schedules and Health Checks, learn how to transform raw operational data into intelligence so that you can diagnose issues quickly, validate hypotheses with confidence, and spot performance anti‑patterns before they impact your business.

    Key takeaways:

    • Initiate investigations into your Job Schedules to identify issues and reduce detection and resolution times from days to minutes
    • Confirm hypotheses, monitor ingestion and segmentation performance, diagnose timing delays, and design smarter schedules that prevent vertical competition
    • Use Health Checks to inspect and detect schemas and identities anti-patterns, understand root causes, and apply best practices for data modeling and quality wins

    , Executive Director, Content Platform Capabilities & Innovation, Eli Lilly and Company

    , Associate VP, Content Capabilities and Innovation, Eli Lilly & Company

    Organizations are transforming their content supply chains with Adobe GenStudio, accelerating the creation of meaningful, personalized content, increasing visibility across marketing workflows, and scaling content operations enterprise-wide. But achieving this transformation requires more than adopting new tools — it demands intentional focus, cross‑functional alignment, and proven operational practices that unlock the full value of AI‑powered content workflows. Whether your organization is just beginning its transformation or is already well underway, learn strategies, best practices, and real‑world insights to help you optimize your content operations and confidently lead your organization into an AI‑powered future.

    Key takeaways:

    • Prepare your organization for a content supply chain transformation by aligning people, processes, and governance around a unified vision for lasting adoption
    • Leverage Adobe GenStudio to drive measurable value and adoption, enabling teams to produce high‑quality content faster and at scale
    • Learn from customer success stories, including how to navigate challenges, remove operational barriers, and realize significant efficiency and performance gains
    Track: Content Supply Chain for the AI World
    Product: Adobe GenStudio

    Level Up Your AI and Agentic Skills in Adobe Journey Optimizer [L535]

    , Principal Product Manager, Adobe

    , Sr. Product Manager, Adobe

    Take your omnichannel journey engagement to the next level with AI-powered tools and agentic workflows in Adobe Journey Optimizer. In this hands-on lab, you’ll learn how to harness advanced AI features, including AI decisioning, experimentation, AI-generated content, and Journey Agent, to streamline journey processes and deliver personalized, optimized journeys at scale. Through guided exercises, attendees will gain practical skills that can be applied immediately to create smarter, more adaptive customer experiences.

    What you will learn:

    • Learn how to use Journey Agent to rapidly create multi-step journeys from prompts.
    • Master techniques for delivering relevant, real-time experiences across channels with AI decisioning and AI-generated content creation.
    • Explore journey simulation and dry run to test journeys.
    • Leverage proactive AI suggestions to surface operational insights and mitigate conflicts and anomalies in your journey workflows.
    Track: Customer Engagement, Orchestrating Experiences with AI Agents

    Mail Drop Ready: Test-Ready Variants, Mail-Ready in Minutes [CP11]

    Direct mail performs best when it’s planned as a touchpoint in an omnichannel journey, not a one-off extension. See how the newly available postal-compliant templates and the Direct Mail Ads add-on in Adobe Express help you turn existing on-brand creative into a mailbox-ready format in minutes, with built-in compliance checks and quality controls. Discover how you can run higher-frequency testing of offers, messaging, creative, and format without creating governance bottlenecks.

    Key takeaways:

    • Learn a self-serve workflow to create, version, and approve mail at speed
    • Coordinate creative, audience, and in-home timing so that mail lands in sync with your cross-channel plan, improving relevance and reducing wasted spend
    • Get a repeatable approach to scale direct mail production, cut handoffs and rework, and accelerate time to market while protecting brand consistency, quality, and control

    By clicking add to schedule, I agree the Adobe family of companies may share my contact information with Deloitte Digital for marketing purposes.

    Making Agentic AI Real: Creative Engineering for Next-Gen CX [S717]

    , Director - MarTech and Web Platforms, Vanguard

    , VP, Head of Digital Experience, HealthEquity

    , Senior Vice President - Marketing Technology & Data, U.S. Bank

    , Vice President, Digital Products, Customer, Infineon

    , Global Head, Digital Experience, Tata Consultancy Services

    Join a cross‑industry panel to explore how agentic AI is redefining customer experience end to end, from marketing and commerce to support and service. The discussion focuses on creative engineering and what’s emerging at the intersection of agentic systems, content, data, and technology, highlighting the growing need for brand discoverability, marketing orchestration, agentic commerce, and proactive support in shaping how experiences are built, found, and optimized. Panelists share perspectives on what this means in practice for brands designing, scaling, and measuring next‑generation customer experiences.

    Key takeaways:

    • From AI experimentation to CX advantage, how to turn agentic AI into real business impact
    • Creative engineering as a growth lever by harmonizing design, content, data, and technology
    • Connecting marketing, commerce, and service journeys for a unified experience

    By clicking add to schedule, I agree the Adobe family of companies may share my contact information with Tata Consultancy Services for marketing purposes.

    Making Agent-to-Agent Marketing Work for Humans  [S816]

    , VP of Product & Strategy, Adobe

    , Global Director, VML Intelligence

    , Global Creative Data Lead, VML

    Explore the emerging agentic web, where AI agents aren’t just tools but an influential consumer segment acting on behalf of the people they represent. From search to purchase to brand reach and loyalty, Emma Chiu and Jason Carmel from VML, and Loni Stark from Adobe, delve into why enterprises need to re-engineer their marketing strategy to leverage machine agents. Learn about the emerging landscape, opportunities, and practical implications for businesses to stay human while catering to algorithmic audiences. At a time when 73% of people believe that “in the future, people and digital humans will live and work alongside one another,” we unpack what that future looks like and what it means for brands, customers, and the evolving relationship between people and machines.

    Key takeaways:

    • Learn how agent-to-agent marketing is already happening and its impact on brand visibility
    • Explore the seemingly inherent compromises and contradictions that follow marketing to machines while keeping human customers at the forefront
    • Define practical steps to ready your brand for agentic marketing without sacrificing human connection
    Track: Commerce, Brand Visibility and Content Management in the Agentic Web, AI, Innovation, and Leadership Trends, Orchestrating Experiences with AI Agents
    Product: Not Product-Specific

    Making AI Stick: Scaling AI Adoption That Drives Lasting Impact [S818]

    , VP, Digital Employee Experience, Adobe

    , Global Vice President of Enterprise Marketing, Adobe

    , Principal, Divisional CIO, Corporate Systems, Vanguard

    As enterprise marketing teams move past AI pilots, the challenge becomes scaling adoption in ways that deliver consistent, measurable value. Success at this stage requires clear operating models, aligned leadership, and the cultural foundations that help teams confidently adopt AI as part of their daily work while enabling innovation. Leaders from Vanguard and Adobe share how they are advancing enterprise AI adoption by turning executive priorities into actionable OKRs, establishing governance and enablement models, supporting teams through change and skill evolution, and removing friction from core workflows to unlock efficiency, performance, and long-term impact.

    Key takeaways:

    • Prioritize high-value AI use cases that deliver measurable impact and enable scale
    • Embed AI into workflows to drive consistent adoption without creating silos
    • Define clear ownership and governance that balance speed, risk, and compliance
    Track: AI, Innovation, and Leadership Trends
    Product: Not Product-Specific

    Manage Connections and Workflows in Adobe Workfront [TRN10]

    , Technical Instructor, Adobe

    Please bring your own device to participate in the hands-on demonstrations during this session.

     

    Manage Connections and Workflows in Adobe Workfront Fusion is a course that walks you through the steps to create scenarios using Workfront Fusion to automate workflows within Adobe Workfront or with external software. You’ll learn about Workfront Fusions basics, including modules and apps, while creating workflows that can connect Workfront to other business-critical applications. This course is designed for Adobe Workfront system administrators or API workflow creators.

    Manage Projects with Adobe Workfront [TRN11]

    , Software Instructor, Adobe

    Please bring your own device to participate in the hands-on demonstrations during this session.

     

    Manage Projects with Adobe Workfront is an introductory course that focuses on creating and managing project timelines, effective collaboration through the project, and monitoring ongoing project work with Adobe Workfront. The course focuses on the main stages of the project lifecycle: Create, Plan, Manage, and Close. Using hands-on exercises and real-world scenarios, we’ll follow a basic workflow that starts with receiving and reviewing a work request before creating and planning a project to manage related work. After launching the project, we’ll look at work progress indicators and how to troubleshoot situations that may arise. Finally, we’ll ensure all work is complete before closing the project. 

    Track: Workflow and Planning
    Product: Adobe Workfront
    Purchase Required: $850 in addition to the required in-person full conference pass Visit Attendee Dashboard.

    Monday, Apr 20
    Marketing for Humans and Machines [CP13]

    , Senior Director, Global Enterprise Marketing, Adobe

    , Director – Marketing Technology, National Football League

    , VP, Brand & DX, Lumen

    , Director, Marketing Craft Development and Capabilities, Intuit

    , Sr. Marketing Lead - Personalization, Sling TV

    For decades, marketing has been designed for human attention—stories, visuals, emotion, and brand. But a new audience has entered the decision-making process: AI agents that research products, compare vendors, summarize information, and increasingly recommend choices on behalf of people.

    This shift raises a fundamental question for enterprise marketers: if both humans and machines are now consuming your content, what should marketing look like?

    In this session, visionary enterprise leaders explore how the rise of AI-mediated decision-making is changing the role of marketing content—from persuasive storytelling to trusted, structured knowledge. The companies that win will be those that can design content ecosystems that resonate emotionally with people while remaining legible, verifiable, and actionable for AI systems.

    The future of marketing isn’t just about capturing human attention. It’s about becoming the most trusted answer for both the human asking the question—and the agent helping them decide.

    Track: AI, Innovation, and Leadership Trends, Orchestrating Experiences with AI Agents
    Product: Not Product-Specific

    Marketo Demon Hunting: Slaying Chaos with Data and Automation [S206]

    , Sr. Manager, Marketo Solution Architect, Qualcomm

    , Solutions Architect - Marketing Technology, Perficient

    In the high stakes world of Marketing Operations, chaos lurks in every corner: rogue data, misaligned audiences, and campaigns that refuse to scale. But like any skilled demon hunter, a marketing automation architect doesn’t just survive the madness — they turn it into performance. Step inside Qualcomm’s MarTech battleground, where executable campaigns act as enchanted blades, data hygiene serves as sacred armor, and audience alignment becomes the choreography that keeps teams in sync. Discover how precision, creativity, and operational mastery helped tame inefficiency and unlock scalable, personalized marketing at enterprise speed. Whether you’re new to the realm of Marketo or a seasoned warrior with years of battle scars, get practical strategies to strengthen your operations and build a more resilient, high-performing marketing engine.

    Key takeaways:

    • The secret to building executable campaigns that cut through complexity
    • Rituals for data hygiene that protect systems and power insight
    • Audience alignment strategies that harmonize personalization across platforms
    Track: B2B Customer Journeys
    Product: Adobe Marketo Engage

    Mastering Marketo Engage AI from Native Gen AI to External API [S208]

    , RevOps AI Engineer, Telynyx

    , Marketing Operations Manager, Sprout Social

    AI is transforming RevOps, but how do you balance rapid adoption with deep customization? This session bridges the gap between Adobe’s native features and the limitless potential of external LLMs. We begin with a deep dive into Adobe Marketo Engage’s newest native capabilities, showcasing the AI Assistant for content generation in the Email Designer and the upcoming Product Knowledge assistant to accelerate your time-to-market. 

    Next, you will learn how to connect Marketo Engage to external AI models using Webhooks and Self-Service Flow Steps and a "PII Isolation" strategy to keep customer data safe while unlocking job title to persona normalization, form fill categorization, and "Hear About Us" attribution data standardization. 

    Key Takeaways: 

    • Maximize Native Tools: Master Marketo Engage's native AI Assistants to streamline email content creation and access product expertise. 

    • Architect for Scale: Learn to securely integrate external LLMs into Marketo Engage workflows using Webhooks and Self-Service Flow Steps. 

    • Deploy Immediately: Walk away with 3 low-risk, high-reward AI blueprints that instantly improve data hygiene and segmentation. 

    Track: B2B Customer Journeys, AI, Innovation, and Leadership Trends
    Product: Adobe Marketo Engage

    Kate Parker
    12:00 p.m. Monday, Apr 20
    Monday, Apr 20
    Maximize AI ROI with Unified Governance [OS754]

    Enterprise AI has reached a turning point, moving from experimentation to measurable ROI. But for most organizations, the biggest blocker isn’t models, it’s whether AI can safely and confidently use consumer data in the first place. User permissions — consumer choices on what you can do with their data across channels, regions, and brands — are a critical input to enterprise AI. When those signals are fragmented across consent banners, CRMs, and ad platforms, AI systems operate on incomplete or outdated data, resulting in smaller audiences, slower journeys, constrained model performance, and under-realized returns on AI and Adobe investments.

    Key takeaways:

    • Discover how unified data governance activates AI ROI, reduces preference lag, and increases audience reach
    • Learn how to unify permissions at the data layer to activate AI with confidence while expanding reach and accelerating personalization
    • Automate governance and establish the foundations for an AI-driven growth
    Track: AI, Innovation, and Leadership Trends
    Product: Adobe Campaign, Adobe Target, Adobe Experience Platform, Adobe Real-Time CDP, Adobe Firefly, Adobe GenAI, Adobe Journey Optimizer B2B Edition, Adobe Experience Platform Agent Orchestrator, Adobe Firefly Foundry, Adobe Firefly Custom Models, Adobe LLM Optimizer
    • Monday, Apr 2012:45 PM - 1:00 PM PDT
      (Tuesday, Apr 211:15 AM - 1:30 AM IST)

    Michelle Cascone
    Monday, Apr 20
    1:00 p.m. Monday, Apr 20
    Maximizing Your Content Supply Chain Investment: Path to Value Realization [CP5]

    , VP, Growth Architect, Merkle

    , EVP - Chief Growth Architect, Alliances, Merkle

    , Head of Marketing Operations and Enablement, Vanguard

    Enterprises have invested billions in content supply chain platforms and AI, yet most are not seeing the returns they expected. The problem is not the technology. It is the organizational change required to enable it: processes built in silos, assets scattered across tools and teams, and no clear metrics connecting platform usage to business outcomes. AI is fundamentally changing how we operate content supply chains, but successful transformation requires change across organizational layers aligned to platform transformation. Understand what separates organizations that realize exceptional value from their platforms versus those that haven’t yet. You'll discover how connecting people, processes, and technology creates the foundation for sustained performance.

    Key takeaways:

    • Why organizational transformation determines platform value realization
    • Breaking through barriers that prevent investment returns
    • The framework for maximizing your platform investment

    Measuring What Matters: Connecting Content to Conversion [L123]

    , Principal Expert Solutions Consultant, Solutions Consulting, Adobe

    , Sr. Product Manager, Digital Experience, Adobe

    For too long, content performance has been measured in a vacuum, with views and clicks disconnected from the actual customer journey. Adobe Content Analytics changes this by connecting what customers see with what they do — engagement, conversion, and revenue. Learn to identify how content impacts the customer journey and which attributes of content, such as colors, background scenes, keywords, and persuasion strategies, influence high-value conversions versus friction. We’ll walk through the new automated configuration wizard that makes it easy to connect content data with customer journey data. Then visualize the data to identify high‑impact assets, diagnose underperforming content patterns, and uncover opportunities for personalization, reuse, and optimization across the customer journey.

    Key takeaways:

    • Learn how to deploy Adobe Content Analytics using an automated configuration flow that unifies content data with customer journey data, all without heavy technical effort
    • Discover which creative attributes accelerate conversion or cause friction
    • Identify opportunities to improve personalization, refresh fatigued assets, and promote winning content
    Track: Analytics
    Product: Adobe Experience Platform, Adobe Experience Cloud, Adobe Content Analytics, Adobe Customer Journey Analytics

    Milliseconds Matter: How to Build for Speed, Scale, and Flexibility [S505]

    , Director, Product Marketing, Adobe Real-Time CDP, Adobe

    , Principal Product Manager, Adobe

    Personalization happens in milliseconds and must perform at the scale of millions of customers. Learn how Adobe Real‑Time CDP is architected to deliver the speed, scale, and flexibility required for modern customer experiences. Learn best practices and considerations for business and technical teams as we go inside the underpinnings of this market-leading CDP. Discover how Adobe Real-Time CDP offers flexibility in integrating with other systems in the tech stack, enabling composable workflows, collaboration with partners, and powerful AI-driven audiences and activation. Get insights from customer deployments about how to enable and manage low-latency use cases with real‑time streaming and edge segmentation combined with always‑on activation.

    Key takeaways: 

    • Plan integration patterns with other systems in your martech stack to power customer experience use cases
    • Make the most of the Real-Time CDP architecture to support real-time ingestion, streaming and edge segmentation, always-on activation, and massive scale
    • Apply proven best practices and practical design patterns to improve performance, agility, and time to value
    Track: Customer Data Management and Collaboration
    Product: Adobe Audience Manager, Adobe Experience Platform, Adobe Real-Time CDP, Adobe Real-Time CDP Collaboration
